ArtifactMetadata.Summary: Plan for building the Manager Dashboard

args.CodeContent:
# Manager Dashboard Implementation Plan

This plan outlines the architecture and steps required to build the new Manager Dashboard page with all 6 reports, role-based visibility, and drill-down functionality.

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Structure and Navigation Setup
- Create a new `<div id="screen-dashboard" class="screen">` inside the `.content-area`.
- Update the "View As" dropdown to call a new `renderManagerDashboard()` function in addition to `renderLeadTable()`, ensuring that role switches re-render the dashboard dynamically.

### 2. Page Header & Layout
- Build a full-width header with the title "Manager Dashboard", the role badge pill ("Viewing as: [Role]"), and the "Export All Reports" button.
- Setup the main content layout: `max-width: 1200px` centered, vertically stacked cards with `24px` gaps and `#F5F4F9` background.

### 3. Role-Based Visibility Engine
- Inside `renderManagerDashboard()`, check `window.CURRENT_ROLE` and `document.getElementById('role-select').value` (if Finance is supported).
  - If IBM Manager / TL: Render all 6 sections.
  - If Finance Team: Render only MIS Reconciliation, Free Look Tracker, and Commission Ledger.

### 4. Reusable Components
- **Section Template Builder:** A helper function to generate the card wrapper, header (title, tags, filters), and body for consistency.
- **Empty State Component:** A helper function returning the standard empty state HTML.
- **Slide-Over Panel:** A shared `<div id="dashboard-slideover">` element for drill-downs. Added to the root level. When a metric is clicked, this panel slides in, showing a filtered lead table with an "Export CSV" button.

### 5. Report Sections Implementation
- **Report 1: Insurance Pipeline Report:** 5 metric summary strip + stage-wise pipeline table.
- **Report 2: Advisor Performance Dashboard:** Agent-level metrics, conversion rate coloring, and team total footer.
- **Report 3: MIS Reconciliation Report:** Reconciliation table showing MIS gaps and matching flags.
- **Report 4: Free Look & Clawback Tracker:** Days remaining urgency coloring and sorting.
- **Report 5: Partner Lead Report:** Partner-level conversion funnel and follow-ups.
- **Report 6: Commission Ledger:** Ledger table calculating expected commission (20%) with payout/payin tracking.

## Open Questions
- The prompt mentions the "Finance Team" role read from the "View As" dropdown. Currently, the dropdown only has "IBM Manager" and "IBM Agent". I will add "Finance Team" as a third option to this dropdown to support the role-based visibility logic. Is that correct?

## Verification Plan
- Switch between Agent, Manager, and Finance roles to ensure the dashboard visibility updates instantly.
- Click metric counts across different tables to verify the slide-over opens with the correct lead data.
- Ensure the layout is responsive and matches the exact specs (1200px max-width, colors, fonts).

args.CodeContent:
# Manager Dashboard Implementation Complete

The **Manager Dashboard** is now fully built and integrated into the Sangam CRM interface. This page offers 6 critical reports dynamically tailored to the user's role.

## Layout & Architecture
- **Single Page Scroll**: All 6 reports stack gracefully with 24px spacing and sit perfectly centered within a max 1200px container on a crisp `#F5F4F9` background.
- **Top Header Bar**: Displays the "Manager Dashboard" title and a pill describing your active role (e.g. "Viewing as: IBM Manager").

## Role-Based Visibility Engine
We’ve added a third role option in the sidebar: **Finance Team**.
Switching between these roles re-renders the dashboard instantly:
- **IBM Manager / TL**: Has full visibility of all 6 operational and financial reports.
- **Finance Team**: Is locked out of operational charts. They ONLY see the 3 reports tailored to them (MIS, Free Look, Commission Ledger).

## Report Details
> [!NOTE]
> All quantitative metrics within the tables and summary strips are fully actionable! Clicking them triggers the slide-over panel.

1. **Insurance Pipeline Report**: Displays the health of the CRM stages with a quick-summary top strip. 
2. **Advisor Performance Dashboard**: Compares agent activity side-by-side. The conversion rate highlights distinctly in green/amber/red depending on their closure performance.
3. **MIS Reconciliation Report**: Quickly spot discrepancies in premiums. Entries that do not match the CRM are flagged instantly with a red ⚠ Gap pill.
4. **Free Look Tracker**: Sorts policies with days remaining ascending to show the most urgent ones first, featuring a visual gradient of red/amber/green days-to-expiry indicators.
5. **Partner Lead Report**: Granular view mapping total leads from external partners like E2E Sales.
6. **Commission Ledger**: Auto-calculates 20% on the paid premium and cleanly aggregates pending payouts vs received payouts.

## Shared Slide-Over Component
A masterfully designed right-side panel that intercepts any click on a metric across any table! It slides in seamlessly via CSS transitions, bringing with it an auto-filtered table to show *exactly* which leads make up that metric. The panel sports an "Export CSV" button alongside the total count.

Feel free to switch roles in the left menu to test the views!

ArtifactMetadata.Summary: Implementation plan for accordion-based history view in the center panel.

args.CodeContent:
# Accordion-Based History View Implementation

This plan outlines the major architectural change required to convert the previous static "Journey Summary" block into dynamic, stage-specific history accordions for the CRM's center panel.

## Proposed Changes

We will refactor the center panel rendering engine to recursively stack read-only historical stages as accordions above the current active stage.

### 1. New Accordion UI Component
We will build a reusable accordion wrapper function:
- **Collapsed View**: A white box with a 1px border (`#E5E7EB`), 10px radius, padding, and a rotating chevron. It will feature a colored stage dot and summary text.
- **Expanded View**: Toggles `display: block/none` on click. It will contain the historical stage's specific HTML structure.

### 2. Historical Content Generators
We will branch the existing rendering functions to support a new `isHistory` mode. When `isHistory = true`:
- All input fields become `readonly` / `disabled` with a muted background (`#F9FAFB`) and `not-allowed` cursor.
- Action buttons ("Generate Quote", "Share Quote", "Share Payment Link") are completely omitted from the DOM.
- Toggles and Checkboxes are locked into their final selected states with reduced opacity.

#### Stage-Specific Mappings:
- **Section 1: New Stage History**
  - **Summary**: "[Product] details filled · [X] fields completed"
  - **Content**: The Product Details Form (e.g. Loan Protection, Property) with all inputs locked. The tab switcher will be hidden; only the actively used tab will render.
- **Section 2: Contacted Stage History**
  - **Summary**: "Quotes generated · [Insurer 1] ₹[Premium] ..."
  - **Content**: All generated quote cards. The "Share" button will be stripped out and replaced with a static `✓ Shared` badge for the shared quote.
- **Section 3: Quote Stage History**
  - **Summary**: "Payment link shared · [Insurer] · ₹[Premium] · [Status]"
  - **Content**: The selected quote card + the Payment Card (link, file upload proof, OCR fields, and verification decision) completely locked down with no buttons.

### 3. Engine Redesign & "Journey Summary" Removal
- The hardcoded "Journey Summary" block currently rendering at the top of the `Payment Done` and `Policy Issued` stages will be completely deleted.
- `renderCenterPanel()` will be updated to intelligently stack the accordions based on the lead's current bucket.
  - *Example:* If lead is in `Quote`, it renders: `Accordion(New)` + `Accordion(Contacted)` + `ActiveView(Quote)`.
  - *Example:* If lead is in `Payment Done`, it renders: `Accordion(New)` + `Accordion(Contacted)` + `Accordion(Quote)` + `ActiveView(Payment Done)`.
  - *Example:* If lead is in `Policy Issued`, it renders: `Accordion(New)` + `Accordion(Contacted)` + `Accordion(Quote)` + `Accordion(Payment Done)` + `ActiveView(Policy Issued)`.

## Open Questions
- When the Agent expands a history accordion, does it stay open if they expand a second one, or should expanding one automatically collapse all others? *(I plan to implement them independently so multiple can be open at once, as mentioned in your prompt. Just verifying no exclusivity is required.)*

## Verification Plan
1. Move a test lead through the funnel from `New` -> `Contacted` -> `Quote` -> `Payment Done`.
2. Visually confirm that the appropriate accordions appear at the top of the center panel at each stage.
3. Expand each accordion and verify that no inputs are editable and no action buttons are present.
4. Verify that the bottom active stage remains fully functional and unaffected by the read-only logic above it.

ArtifactMetadata.Summary: Walkthrough of the new Accordion-based history UI

args.CodeContent:
# Center Panel Accordion History Implementation

I have fully refactored the CRM's Center Panel to use a dynamic, accordion-based history view. The old, static "Journey Summary" has been completely removed in favor of this scalable design.

## Structural Changes

1. **New Accordion UI Component (`renderAccordion`)**
   - Built a robust, reusable component for rendering collapsed historical stages.
   - Features include a colored stage indicator, a one-line summary tailored to the specific stage, and a toggleable expansion behavior that allows multiple accordions to be open at once.

2. **Stage Generators Refactored**
   - **`renderProductTabsForm` (New Stage)**: Modified to accept an `isHistory` flag. When true, it locks all form inputs (`disabled`, `readonly`, muted background, `not-allowed` cursor), hides the tab switcher (showing only the active product), and removes the "Generate Quote" button.
   - **`renderContactedStage` (Contacted Stage)**: Separated from the old Quote stage. Now purely handles the display of generated quotes. In history mode, it strips out the "Share Quote" buttons and dynamically adds a `✓ Shared` badge to the plan that was selected by the customer.
   - **`renderQuoteStage` (Quote Stage)**: Now focuses strictly on the selected customer plan and the Payment generation/verification process. In history mode, it strips out the "Resend Link", "Verify Payment", and "Extract with AI" buttons, leaving only a locked-down view of the OCR data and verified status.
   - **`renderPaymentStage` (Payment Done Stage)**: Updated to strip out all interactive milestone decision buttons (Approve, Counter Offer, Reject, Confirm Upload) when rendered as history (which occurs when the lead reaches the `Policy Issued` bucket).

3. **`renderCenterPanel` Engine Overhaul**
   - Ripped out the old sequential `if/else` rendering block.
   - Replaced it with a progressive history stack. The engine now calculates the index of the lead's current bucket. For every stage *before* the current bucket, it dynamically generates an Accordion block with the read-only version of that stage's UI.
   - The active stage remains at the bottom of the stack, completely editable.

### Visual Example (Payment Done Bucket)
When a lead reaches "Payment Done", the center panel perfectly stacks:
- **New** (Accordion, collapsed)
- **Contacted** (Accordion, collapsed)
- **Quote** (Accordion, collapsed)
- **Payment Done** (Active milestone tracker, fully interactive)

Everything is live and ready for testing in your browser!

args.ReplacementContent:
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
  <title>Ambak | Simplifying Home Finance</title>
  <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Poppins:wght@400;500;600;700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
  <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js"></script>
  <script>
    window.onerror = function(msg, url, lineNo, columnNo, error) {
      alert("GLOBAL ERROR: " + msg + " \nLine: " + lineNo + " \nCol: " + columnNo);
      let errDiv = document.createElement('div');
      errDiv.style.cssText = 'position:fixed; top:0; left:0; width:100%; padding:20px; background:red; color:white; z-index:99999; font-weight:bold; font-family:monospace; white-space:pre-wrap;';
      errDiv.innerText = "CRASH: " + msg + "\nLine: " + lineNo + "\n" + (error && error.stack ? error.stack : '');
      document.body.appendChild(errDiv);
      return false;
    };
    window.onunhandledrejection = function(event) {
      alert("UNHANDLED PROMISE REJECTION: " + event.reason);
      let errDiv = document.createElement('div');
      errDiv.style.cssText = 'position:fixed; top:0; left:0; width:100%; padding:20px; background:darkred; color:white; z-index:99999; font-weight:bold; font-family:monospace; white-space:pre-wrap;';
      errDiv.innerText = "PROMISE CRASH: " + event.reason + "\n" + (event.reason && event.reason.stack ? event.reason.stack : '');
      document.body.appendChild(errDiv);
    };
  </script>
  <style>

ArtifactMetadata.Summary: Implementation Plan for updating state machine logic and UI dropdown filtering for New, Contacted, and Quote buckets.

args.CodeContent:
# Implement Complete Status/Sub-Status State Machine Logic

This plan outlines the changes to implement the requested state machine logic across the NEW, CONTACTED, and QUOTE buckets.

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Update `STATE_MACHINE_CONFIG`
We will update the `STATE_MACHINE_CONFIG` in `index.html` to reflect the exact statuses, sub-statuses, and follow-up rules provided for the three buckets:
- **NEW Bucket**: `Not Connected`, `Call Back`, `Not Interested` (and the hidden `Interested`)
- **CONTACTED Bucket**: `Call Back`, `Not Interested` (and the hidden `Quote Shared`)
- **QUOTE Bucket**: `Quote Shared`, `Payment Link Shared`, `Payment Done`

To support the requirement that auto-triggered statuses/sub-statuses do NOT appear in manual dropdowns, we will add an `autoOnly` property to specific statuses and sub-statuses in the configuration. 

> [!IMPORTANT]
> The configuration objects provided in the prompt for `QUOTE_STATUSES` include sub-statuses like "Awaiting Payment" and "Resend Payment Link", which are auto-triggered, and "Payment Done" which is entirely auto-triggered. 
> To strictly follow the rule *"Any status/sub-status combination that gets set via an automatic center panel trigger should NOT appear as a selectable option in the dropdown"*, we will flag these as `autoOnly` in the configuration so they are filtered out of the dropdown UI.

### 2. Update Right Panel UI (`renderRightPanel` & `openStatusModal`)
- Modify the `statusOptionsHtml` generation loop to skip any status where `config.autoOnly === true`.
- Modify the `subStatus` dropdown loop to skip any sub-status where `subStatus === 'Consent Pending'`, `'Awaiting Payment'`, `'Resend Payment Link'`, `'Verification Pending'`, `'Verified'`, or `'Verification Failed'` (or by checking an `autoOnlySubStatuses` array).
- Ensure that if `followUpRequired` (currently `mandatoryFollowUp`) is true, the Follow-Up Date and Pending With fields are shown and mandatory.
- Use the updated `pendingWith` arrays from the prompt to populate the "Pending With" dropdown dynamically, rather than using a static list.

### 3. Implement Automatic Triggers
We will ensure the center panel actions automatically set the status, sub-status, and log the activity history as requested:

- **NEW Bucket**:
  - Clicking "Generate Quote" or "Open PolicyBazaar": auto-sets `Interested` > `Need Quote`, logs activity, moves to Contacted.
- **CONTACTED Bucket**:
  - Clicking "Share Quote" or "Share All Quotes": auto-sets `Quote Shared` > `Consent Pending`, logs activity, moves to Quote.
- **QUOTE Bucket**:
  - Clicking "Share Payment Link": auto-sets `Payment Link Shared` > `Awaiting Payment`, logs activity, stays in Quote.
  - Clicking "Resend Payment Link": auto-sets `Payment Link Shared` > `Resend Payment Link`, logs activity, stays in Quote.
  - Clicking "Send for Verification" (agent): auto-sets `Payment Done` > `Verification Pending`, logs activity, stays in Quote.
  - Clicking "Mark as Verified" (manager): auto-sets `Payment Done` > `Verified`, logs activity, moves to Payment Done.
  - Clicking "Verification Failed" (manager): auto-sets `Payment Done` > `Verification Failed`, logs activity, stays in Quote.

## User Review Required
> [!WARNING]
> Please review the interpretation of the `QUOTE_STATUSES` config. I will add logic to actively hide "Awaiting Payment", "Resend Payment Link", and the entire "Payment Done" status from the manual dropdowns, even though they were listed in the `QUOTE_STATUSES` configuration snippet. Let me know if this aligns with your expectations!

## Verification Plan
1. **Manual Selection**: Verify that the dropdowns in the right panel only show the manually selectable options.
2. **Auto Triggers**: Click through the center panel actions (Generate Quote, Share Quote, Share Payment Link, Send for Verification) and verify that the "Current Status" bar and Activity History update automatically without requiring manual dropdown selection.

args.CodeContent:
# Implement Complete Status/Sub-Status State Machine Logic

This plan outlines the changes to implement the requested state machine logic across the NEW, CONTACTED, and QUOTE buckets.

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Update `STATE_MACHINE_CONFIG`
We will update the `STATE_MACHINE_CONFIG` in `index.html` to reflect the exact statuses, sub-statuses, and follow-up rules provided for the three buckets:
- **NEW Bucket**: `Not Connected`, `Call Back`, `Not Interested` (and the hidden `Interested`)
- **CONTACTED Bucket**: `Call Back`, `Not Interested` (and the hidden `Quote Shared`)
- **QUOTE Bucket**: `Quote Shared`, `Payment Link Shared`, `Payment Done`

To support the requirement that auto-triggered statuses/sub-statuses do NOT appear in manual dropdowns, we will add an `autoOnly` property to specific statuses and sub-statuses in the configuration. 

> [!IMPORTANT]
> The configuration objects provided in the prompt for `QUOTE_STATUSES` include sub-statuses like "Awaiting Payment" and "Resend Payment Link", which are auto-triggered, and "Payment Done" which is entirely auto-triggered. 
> To strictly follow the rule *"Any status/sub-status combination that gets set via an automatic center panel trigger should NOT appear as a selectable option in the dropdown"*, we will flag these as `autoOnly` in the configuration so they are filtered out of the dropdown UI.

### 2. Update Right Panel UI (`renderRightPanel` & `openStatusModal`)
- Modify the `statusOptionsHtml` generation loop to skip any status where `config.autoOnly === true`.
- Modify the `subStatus` dropdown loop to skip any sub-status where `subStatus === 'Consent Pending'`, `'Awaiting Payment'`, `'Resend Payment Link'`, `'Verification Pending'`, `'Verified'`, or `'Verification Failed'` (or by checking an `autoOnlySubStatuses` array).
- Ensure that if `followUpRequired` (currently `mandatoryFollowUp`) is true, the Follow-Up Date and Pending With fields are shown and mandatory.
- Use the updated `pendingWith` arrays from the prompt to populate the "Pending With" dropdown dynamically, rather than using a static list.

### 3. Implement Automatic Triggers
We will ensure the center panel actions automatically set the status, sub-status, and log the activity history as requested:

- **NEW Bucket**:
  - Clicking "Generate Quote" or "Open PolicyBazaar": auto-sets `Interested` > `Need Quote`, logs activity, moves to Contacted.
- **CONTACTED Bucket**:
  - Clicking "Share Quote" or "Share All Quotes": auto-sets `Quote Shared` > `Consent Pending`, logs activity, moves to Quote.
- **QUOTE Bucket**:
  - Clicking "Share Payment Link": auto-sets `Payment Link Shared` > `Awaiting Payment`, logs activity, stays in Quote.
  - Clicking "Resend Payment Link": auto-sets `Payment Link Shared` > `Resend Payment Link`, logs activity, stays in Quote.
  - Clicking "Send for Verification" (agent): auto-sets `Payment Done` > `Verification Pending`, logs activity, stays in Quote.
  - Clicking "Mark as Verified" (manager): auto-sets `Payment Done` > `Verified`, logs activity, moves to Payment Done.
  - Clicking "Verification Failed" (manager): auto-sets `Payment Done` > `Verification Failed`, logs activity, stays in Quote.

## User Review Required
> [!WARNING]
> Please review the interpretation of the `QUOTE_STATUSES` config. I will add logic to actively hide "Awaiting Payment", "Resend Payment Link", and the entire "Payment Done" status from the manual dropdowns, even though they were listed in the `QUOTE_STATUSES` configuration snippet. Let me know if this aligns with your expectations!

## Verification Plan
1. **Manual Selection**: Verify that the dropdowns in the right panel only show the manually selectable options.
2. **Auto Triggers**: Click through the center panel actions (Generate Quote, Share Quote, Share Payment Link, Send for Verification) and verify that the "Current Status" bar and Activity History update automatically without requiring manual dropdown selection.

args.CodeContent:
# State Machine Updates Walkthrough

I have successfully updated the status state machine logic across the New, Contacted, and Quote buckets to match your strict rules.

## What changed?

1. **State Machine Config Restructure**
   - Implemented exact structures for `NEW_STATUSES`, `CONTACTED_STATUSES`, and `QUOTE_STATUSES`.
   - Added an `autoOnly` flag logic to statuses and sub-statuses that should only ever be triggered programmatically by the system rather than chosen manually.
   
2. **Right Panel Dropdowns Filtered**
   - The dropdown UI in the right panel and the Update Status modal has been re-wired to filter out any status tagged as `autoOnly` and any sub-status tagged as `autoOnlySubStatuses`. 
   - Now, agents can no longer manually select "Payment Done" or auto-triggered statuses like "Quote Shared — Consent Pending" out of sequence! 
   - The "Pending With" dropdown is now dynamically populated based on the array defined in the state config for the selected sub-status.
   
3. **Automatic System Triggers**
   - Programmed the center-panel action buttons to update the status in the background:
     - **Generate Quote** or **Open PolicyBazaar** → `Interested` / `Need Quote`
     - **Share Quote** / **Share All Quotes** → `Quote Shared` / `Consent Pending`
     - **Share Payment Link** → `Payment Link Shared` / `Awaiting Payment`
     - **Resend Payment Link** → `Payment Link Shared` / `Resend Payment Link`
     - **Send for Verification** → `Payment Done` / `Verification Pending`
     - **Manager: Verify Payment** → `Payment Done` / `Verified`
     - **Manager: Verification Failed** → `Payment Done` / `Verification Failed`
   - All these actions write the exact, requested system log entries straight into the Activity History!

You can test this out immediately by clicking through the quote flow and observing how the "Current Status" bar behaves vs what is actually selectable in the Update Status dropdown!

args.CodeContent:
# Complete Chronological Audit Trail Implementation

This plan details how we will migrate the existing activity logging system to the new structured format and dynamically render the "Activity History" tab using the newly designed activity card.

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Update the `Activity History` Renderer
Currently, `getRightPanelTabContent('History')` returns a single, hardcoded static HTML block that serves as the design template for the new activity card. 
- We will update this function to map over the `currentLead.log` array.
- For each log entry, we will inject the structured data (`type`, `actor`, `title`, `description`, `timestamp`, etc.) into the template.
- We will dynamically color-code the `type` tag based on its value (e.g., System Update = gray/amber, Agent Update = blue, IBM Manager Update = green).

### 2. Update `NEW BUCKET` Log Entries
We will replace the existing `currentLead.log.unshift` calls with the new structured object format:
- **Manual Status Updates** (in `saveDetailStatusUpdate`): Check if the lead is in the NEW bucket and log the "Agent Update" with the exact sub-status and remarks.
- **Auto-triggers** (Generate Quote / Open PolicyBazaar): Update `generateQuoteNew` and `openPolicyBazaarFlow` to log the "System Update" moving the lead to Contacted.
- **Mark as Lost** (in `confirmLost`): Log the "Agent Update" with the reason.

### 3. Update `CONTACTED BUCKET` Log Entries
- **Generate Quotes** (in `generateQuoteContacted` / `openPolicyBazaarFlow`): Log the "Agent Update" containing the generated product or PB workspace action.
- **Quote Shared (Single vs. All)** (in `shareQuote`): Differentiate the single insurer share vs. "All" and log the corresponding "Agent Update".
- **Auto-trigger**: Update the automatic "Quote Shared - Consent Pending" to use the structured format.
- **Product Changed** (in `selectProduct` or right panel update): Catch the change and log the "Agent Update".

### 4. Update `QUOTE BUCKET` Log Entries
- **Customer Plan Selection** (in `selectPaymentQuote`): Log the selected plan and premium.
- **Payment Link Shared / Resent** (in `sharePaymentLink`): Log whether it's a first share or resend, along with the auto-status update details.
- **Payment Proof Sent for Verification** (in `sendPaymentVerification`): Log the OCR-captured details (Amount, UTR, Date).
- **Payment Verified / Failed (Manager actions)** (in `managerVerifyPayment` / `confirmManagerFail`): Log the "IBM Manager Update" with the decision and reason.

## Open Questions
> [!NOTE]
> The current codebase relies on older `{ dot, text, time }` logs for some actions outside the immediate NEW, CONTACTED, and QUOTE buckets (e.g., Payment Done, Policy Issued stages). 
> **Question**: Should I implement a fallback renderer in `getRightPanelTabContent('History')` so that if an older-format log object is found, it renders a simplified card? Or is it acceptable if those break/don't render until we migrate the rest of the buckets? I will add a fallback renderer to be safe unless instructed otherwise.

## Verification Plan
- Create a test lead.
- Perform all actions from NEW → CONTACTED → QUOTE → Payment verification.
- Switch to the Activity History tab and confirm every entry matches the exact required format and renders perfectly using the new card design.

args.CodeContent:
# Activity History Implementation

We've completely overhauled the Activity History system! The system now tracks every single user action and system auto-trigger dynamically using the detailed, structured format you requested.

## Changes Made

### 1. Dynamic UI Rendering
- The `History` tab in the right panel (`getRightPanelTabContent`) now dynamically renders the `currentLead.log` array instead of displaying the static placeholder HTML.
- It leverages the existing clean UI components, mapping data like `type`, `actor`, `title`, `description`, and `timestamp` directly to the correct HTML nodes.
- Added a fallback renderer so that any legacy log events (like those generated in the Policy Issued stage) won't break the UI.

### 2. Standardized Log Data Structure
Every action in the NEW, CONTACTED, and QUOTE buckets now pushes a structured object containing all 5 required data points (and metadata). 
The tag colors are dynamically assigned based on the update type:
- `System Update` (Gray / Amber accents)
- `Agent Update` (Blue)
- `IBM Manager Update` (Green)

### 3. Wired Triggers Across All Buckets
We hooked into the following actions to ensure they record precise, immutable logs:
- **Manual Status Updates**: Records the precise sub-status and any remarks/pending with/counter-offer data.
- **Generate Quote / Open PolicyBazaar**: Records the Agent Update (quotes generated/workspace opened) AND the System Update (status auto-updated to Interested — Need Quote).
- **Share Quote**: Captures both single-insurer shares and "Share All" (e.g., "2 quotes shared via WhatsApp & Email"), plus the System auto-update.
- **Plan Selection**: Logs when a customer selects a plan, and even captures when they *change* their plan selection from one insurer to another.
- **Share Payment Link**: Distinguishes between the first share (logs System auto-update + Agent share action) and a resend action.
- **Payment Verification**: Captures the exact OCR details uploaded by the agent (Amount, UTR, Date), and subsequently logs the manager's Approve/Reject decisions.
- **Mark as Lost**: Captures the exact reason and notes, noting the stage the lead was in when it was marked lost.

## Validation Results
- The static hardcoded HTML is gone. Activity histories now dynamically reflect the exact journey of a lead.
- The logs are inserted using `unshift()`, guaranteeing they render chronologically (newest-first).
- The data shape perfectly matches the requested JSON structure, creating a reliable audit trail for agents and managers alike.
args.Description: Create Walkthrough summarizing the Activity History implementation

args.CodeContent:
# PolicyBazaar Quote Upload Flow Implementation

This plan outlines the UI states and logic to handle the PolicyBazaar quote generation and extraction flow for PB-partnered products (Credit Life, Term Plan, Property Insurance).

## Proposed Changes

We will update the `Contacted` stage UI (`renderContactedStage`) to be stateful when dealing with a PolicyBazaar product. 

When the user clicks "Open PolicyBazaar", the following sequence of UI states will be rendered:

### State 1: Awaiting Upload (`window.pbQuoteFlowState === 'opened'`)
- **UI Element**: An upload dropzone/box indicating that the PB workspace is open in a new tab.
- **Message**: "Generate quotes on PolicyBazaar, download the PDF, and upload it here."
- **Action**: A file input allowing the agent to upload the PDF. Selecting a file triggers `simulatePBQuoteUpload(input)`.

### State 2: Uploaded & Ready for Extraction (`window.pbQuoteFlowState === 'uploaded'`)
- **UI Element**: Displays the uploaded file name (`window.pbUploadedQuoteFile`).
- **Action**: A prominent "Extract Quotes using AI ✨" button. Clicking this triggers `extractPBQuotesWithAI()`.

### State 3: Extracting (`window.pbQuoteFlowState === 'extracting'`)
- **UI Element**: A loading spinner or skeleton UI simulating AI processing for 1.5 seconds.

### State 4: Extracted & Ready to Share (`window.pbQuoteFlowState === 'extracted'`)
- **UI Element**: Renders the standard "Generated Quotes" cards (similar to Loan Protection).
- **Action**: "Share All Quotes" or individual "Share Quote" buttons that proceed to the `Quote` stage.

## Activity History Updates
We will inject new activity logs into this flow to ensure chronological tracking:
- When the PDF is uploaded: `Agent Update: PolicyBazaar quote PDF uploaded: [filename]`.
- When quotes are extracted: `System Update: AI successfully extracted 2 quotes from PDF`.

## Open Questions
> [!NOTE]
> Currently, the system uses "CARE Health" and "TATA AIG" as the mocked generated quotes. Should we use different mocked insurer names (e.g., "HDFC Life", "ICICI Pru") for the extracted PolicyBazaar quotes to distinguish them, or keep the existing mocked data? I will keep the existing mock data to minimize changes unless you specify otherwise.

args.CodeContent:
# PolicyBazaar Quote Upload Flow

I've implemented the complete PolicyBazaar Quote Upload flow for the Credit Life, Term Plan, and Property Insurance products! 

## Flow Breakdown

The `Contacted` stage UI is now stateful and reacts to exactly where the agent is in the PolicyBazaar process:

### 1. Awaiting Upload
Once the agent clicks "Open PolicyBazaar" from the New bucket, the UI transitions to an awaiting state. 
- We display a clear message indicating that the PolicyBazaar workspace is open in another tab.
- We show an **"Upload Quote PDF"** button (a file input) that allows the agent to upload the quotes they generated and downloaded from PolicyBazaar.

### 2. Uploaded & Ready for Extraction
When the agent selects a PDF file, the state transitions automatically.
- The UI acknowledges the successful upload and displays the selected filename.
- A prominent **"Extract Quotes using AI ✨"** button is revealed.
- **Activity History:** A new `Agent Update` log is created recording that the PolicyBazaar quote PDF was uploaded, noting the specific filename.

### 3. Extracting (Processing)
Clicking the extract button transitions the UI into a processing state.
- A loading spinner and status message ("Extracting Quotes... Our AI is analyzing the PDF to extract the plans") appear for a short duration to simulate the backend extraction process.

### 4. Extracted & Ready to Share
After extraction completes, the state transitions to the final step in the Contacted bucket.
- The extracted quotes are populated into the standard quote cards we use for all products.
- The "Share All Quotes" and individual "Share Quote" buttons appear, allowing the agent to send them to the customer and move the lead into the `Quote` stage.
- **Activity History:** A new `System Update` log is created stating that the AI successfully extracted the quotes from the PDF.

## Validation
- The UI handles the multi-step transitions smoothly within the same Contacted view.
- The Activity History perfectly captures the chronological steps (Open PB -> Upload PDF -> AI Extract Quotes -> Share Quotes).
- The transition between the `Contacted` and `Quote` buckets is preserved seamlessly.

args.CodeContent:
# Insurance Lead Listing Page Redesign

I've completely redesigned the Lead Listing page to match your new, softer aesthetic and modernized the entire layout and interaction model!

## 1. Visual Refresh (The "Softer" Direction)
The harsh contrasts and bold primary colors have been completely dialed down in favor of a modern, premium look:
- **Backgrounds**: The main page sits on a very light lavender-gray (`#F7F6FB`). The tables use white with alternating near-white lavender tint (`#FDFCFF`) rows.
- **Table Headers**: Soft lavender (`#EEEAF8`) with `473391` text replaces the old dark navy headers.
- **Borders & Hover**: All borders use a soft `#E8E4F3` color, and hovering over a row highlights it with a subtle `#F5F3FD` background.
- **Pills**: The stage status pills have been converted to pastel, desaturated versions of their old colors (e.g., New is `#EEE8FD`, Lost is `#FFE8E8`).

## 2. Page Header & Actions
The header has been cleaned up. "My Insurance Leads" sits prominently on the left. The actions on the right have been styled distinctly:
- **Bulk Assign / Bulk Upload**: Secondary buttons with an outlined `473391` border.
- **Create Insurance Lead**: Primary solid `473391` button.

## 3. Prominent Stage Tabs
The core navigation for your leads is now driven by a full-width row of **Stage Tabs** just below the header. 
- You can instantly see how many leads are in each bucket (e.g., `New (12)`).
- Clicking a tab immediately filters the table.
- The active tab lights up with its specific pastel stage color.

## 4. Inline Filters
The cumbersome "More Filters" side drawer is gone! All critical filters are now available inline for immediate access.
- **Dropdowns**: Product, Source, Status, Assigned Agent (for managers), Follow-up status, and Created Date range.
- **Search**: A dedicated inline search bar for Name, Phone, or Lead ID.
- **Dynamic**: The "Clear Filters" button dynamically appears only when a filter is active, allowing a 1-click reset. 
- **Sorting**: Added a prominent "Sort by" dropdown alongside clickable column headers (with up/down sorting arrows).

## 5. Overhauled Data Table
The table has been rebuilt to be significantly more readable and scannable:
- **Column Sizing**: Widths are rigidly defined for a clean grid.
- **Two-line Data**: We aggressively utilize two-line cells to save horizontal space (e.g., Customer Name + Phone, Bank + Loan Stage).
- **Overdue Visuals**: Overdue follow-ups are called out with a bold red date and a subtle red border on the left edge of the row itself.
- **Missing Data**: Instead of harsh red "missing" text, empty data points gracefully fall back to a soft `—` dash.

Check out the page and let me know if the new aesthetic meets your vision!

ArtifactMetadata.Summary: Implementation plan for the PolicyBazaar Extracted Quote Cards and Inline Edit logic

args.CodeContent:
# Implement PB Extracted Quote Cards

This plan covers redesigning the PolicyBazaar extracted quotes for Property, Credit Life, and Term Plan, incorporating the new compact layout, inline editing, and OCR badge features.

## Proposed Changes

### [index.html]

1. **Mock Data Initialization**:
   - I'll create a global object `window.extractedPBQuotes` to store the OCR-extracted quotes with their values and OCR status flags.
   - Example mock data:
     - **Property**: Bajaj Allianz (₹12,500), HDFC ERGO (₹13,200)
     - **Credit Life**: ICICI Pru (₹4,500/month), HDFC Life (₹52,000/year)
     - **Term Plan**: Max Life (₹1,200/month), TATA AIA (₹14,000/year)
   - Each chip's value will track an `isOcr` boolean (default `true`) which controls the OCR badge.

2. **Render Logic (`renderContactedStage`)**:
   - I will add a new branch `else if (window.pbQuoteFlowState === 'extracted')` in `renderContactedStage()` to specifically handle the PB extracted cards.
   - The layout will identically mirror the Loan Protection cards (white bg, flex row, 1px `#E8E4F3` border, 3px `#6B5CC4` accent bar for all PB cards).
   - I will dynamically inject the correct Labels (e.g., `MARKET VALUE`, `AMOUNT INSURED`, etc.) depending on `window.activeNewBucketTab`.

3. **Inline Editing & OCR Badges**:
   - Each card will have an "Edit" link at the bottom.
   - Clicking "Edit" will re-render the card in "Edit Mode", swapping the static 3 data chips for 3 small text inputs (with `#473391` borders).
   - Clicking "Done" will save the new values, set `isOcr = false` for any changed fields, and re-render the card in "View Mode".
   - The tiny `OCR` badge (font-size 9px, `#EEE8FD` bg, `#473391` color) will render conditionally next to each chip's value based on the `isOcr` flag.

4. **Share Selected Quotes Button**:
   - I will add checkboxes to the top-left of the PB cards.
   - Selecting checkboxes updates the button text: "Share 2 of 3 Selected".
   - Clicking the button will trigger the WhatsApp/Email popup (`shareQuote` function).

## Open Questions
- Do you want the edit functionality to allow editing the *Premium* amount as well, or just the 3 data chips in the middle? (I will assume just the 3 data chips for now based on the prompt).

args.CodeContent:
# PolicyBazaar Quote Cards Redesign

I've completed the redesign of the extracted PB quote cards! They now match the compact, professional layout of the Loan Protection quotes, while introducing advanced extraction and editing capabilities for Property, Term Plan, and Credit Life insurance.

## 1. Compact & Scannable Layout
- The cards use the exact same clean, single-row design: **Identity → Key Details → Premium → Actions**.
- Each PB quote card features a universal `#6B5CC4` (purple) left accent bar, establishing a consistent identity for all PB partner quotes.

## 2. Dynamic Product Details
The center data chips now adapt dynamically based on the product:
- **Property Insurance**: MARKET VALUE | PAYMENT TERM | PREMIUM
- **Credit Life**: AMOUNT INSURED | TENURE | PAYMENT TERM (with Premium frequency displayed as `/month` or `/year`)
- **Term Plan**: AMOUNT INSURED | TENURE | PAYMENT TERM (with Premium frequency displayed as `/month` or `/year`)

## 3. Interactive OCR Editing
- **OCR Badges**: When the AI extracts data from the PB document, every extracted data chip gets a tiny `OCR` badge to visually indicate the source.
- **Inline Editing**: Clicking "Edit values" at the bottom of the card transforms the data chips into text inputs, allowing the agent to correct any OCR mistakes directly within the card.
- **Auto-Update**: Clicking "Done" saves the changes and instantly removes the `OCR` badge from any modified fields, signifying agent verification.

## 4. Multi-Select & Sharing
- **Checkboxes**: Added selection checkboxes to the top-left of every PB card.
- **Share Button**: The "Share Selected Quotes" button dynamically updates to show the selected count (e.g., "Share 1 of 2 Selected") and prevents sharing if none are selected.

Give the PolicyBazaar flow a test run and check out the new inline editing mode!

ArtifactMetadata.Summary: Implementation plan for the 2-Row Filter Bar redesign

args.CodeContent:
# Filter Bar Redesign Implementation Plan

I will restructure the Lead Listing page's filter UI into a comprehensive, always-visible, 2-row system, and significantly expand the filtering and sorting logic behind it.

## 1. UI Updates (HTML & CSS)
### Filter Row 1 (Primary)
- I will inject the new **Insurer** dropdown before the Search field. Options will be dynamically derived or hardcoded as per the prompt (`All Insurers`, `CARE Health`, `TATA AIG`, etc.).

### Filter Row 2 (Secondary)
- I will create a new flex-row container (`.listing-filters-row2`) below the primary row.
- **Styling**: Background `#F7F6FB`, padding `10px 28px`, border-bottom `#E8E4F3`, flex, gap 10px.
- **Filters added**: `HL Stage`, `Bank`, `Loan Amount`, `Premium Range`, `Pending With`, `Payment Status`, `Lead Age`, and `Status Updated`.
- **Right side**: A dynamically rendered purple badge for "Active filters" count, and the unified "Clear All" text link. The existing "Clear Filters" button in Row 1 will be removed.

### Sort Dropdown
- I will replace the existing sort options with the 10 expanded options (e.g., `Follow-up: Overdue First`, `Premium: High to Low`, `Loan Amount: Low to High`).

## 2. Logic Updates (JavaScript)
### Filtering Logic (`getFilteredLeads`)
- **Insurer**: Filters based on `l.finalInsurer` or extracted mock quotes.
- **HL Stage / Bank / Loan Amount**: Extracts data from `l.hlLead` to apply these filters. For amount ranges, I'll parse the SI value.
- **Premium Range**: Extracts the total premium (either from PB/Policy quotes or generic).
- **Pending With**: Simple string match against a new derived or explicit state (if missing, I'll default to 'All').
- **Payment Status**: Filters based on payment status if it exists.
- **Lead Age**: Calculates days since `l.createdAt` to fit into buckets (<7, 7-30, 30-60, 60+).
- **Status Updated**: Checks `l.statusUpdatedAt` (or last activity log date) to match the selected time range.

### Sorting Logic
- I will implement custom sort comparators for the new Premium and Loan Amount fields, ensuring numeric sorting rather than string sorting. Overdue follow-ups will be prioritized when the relevant sort is selected.

### Dynamic States & Interactions
- **Payment Status Disable Rule**: In `setListingStage()` (when a tab is clicked), I will check the stage. If it's "New" or "Contacted", I'll add `opacity: 0.4` and `pointer-events: none` to the Payment Status filter dropdown, along with a custom title/tooltip.
- **Results Summary**: `renderLeadTable()` will be updated to gather the selected text of every active dropdown and append them to the "Showing X leads" string inline.
- **Active Badge / Clear All**: `applyListingFilters()` will count all non-default values across both rows to update the "X active" badge and toggle the visibility of "Clear All".

## Open Questions
- The filter "Pending With" options (Customer, IBM Agent, PolicyBazaar, Insurer, Ambak Operations, Finance Team). Our current mock data doesn't explicitly track a `pendingWith` field. I will filter based on `l.pendingWith` if it exists, otherwise it will just return all for now. Does that sound good?

args.CodeContent: # Listing Filters & History UI Updates I have completed the UI and logic updates for both the Filter Bar and the New Stage History block! ## 1. 2-Row Always-Visible Filter Bar The filter bar has been completely redesigned from a hidden drawer into a powerful, 2-row inline component: - **Row 1**: Houses your primary filters (`Product`, `Source`, `Status`, `Agent`, `Follow-ups`, `Time`, and the new **Insurer** dropdown). - **Row 2**: Introduces 8 powerful new filters directly on screen (`HL Stage`, `Bank`, `Loan Amount`, `Premium Range`, `Pending With`, `Payment Status`, `Lead Age`, `Status Updated`). - **Interactive Rules**: The `Payment Status` filter automatically grays out with a tooltip if you are viewing leads in the "New" or "Contacted" tabs (since payment data isn't relevant there). - **Active Badges & Clearing**: The right side of Row 2 features a purple `[N] active` badge tracking your active filters, and a unified `Clear All` button that resets both rows instantly. ## 2. Dynamic Results Summary The "Showing X leads" text below the filters now explicitly tells you exactly what data you are looking at. When filters are applied, the summary will dynamically output: > Showing 4 leads · Filtered by: Stage: Contacted · Loan Protection · CARE Health · Below ₹25L ## 3. Expanded Sorting Logic The "Sort by" dropdown now supports deep numeric and chronological sorting: - `Premium: High to Low` / `Low to High` - `Loan Amount: High to Low` / `Low to High` - `Lead Age: Oldest First` - `Follow-up: Earliest First` / `Overdue First` ## 4. History Block Formatting Fixed I have also resolved the visual discrepancy in the "Contacted" stage! The historical snapshot of the "New" stage now renders identically to the active "New" stage. You will see all the product tabs (`Loan Protection`, `Property Insurance`, etc.) and the form inputs retain their standard, editable styling rather than appearing grayed out.

ArtifactMetadata.Summary: Implementation Plan for Policy Issued redesign, Policy Cancelled stage, and MIS & Payouts section.

args.CodeContent:
# Policy Lifecycle & MIS Payouts Implementation

This plan outlines the architecture for the three major changes requested: redesigning the Policy Issued bucket, adding a new Policy Cancelled stage, and creating a robust MIS & Payouts dashboard.

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Policy Issued Bucket Redesign
The current dual-card layout for Freelook and MIS tracking will be replaced with a single, system-driven **Freelook Status Tracker**. 

- **State 1: Under Freelook (Amber)**
  - Dynamic countdown and progress bar based on `FREELOOK_DAYS`.
  - Includes a "Request Cancellation" button.
- **State 2: Under Verification (Blue)**
  - Triggered automatically when freelook days expire.
  - Read-only for Agents.
  - Managers see a "Confirm Freelook Completed" button (enabled only if the MIS has been uploaded in the MIS dashboard).
- **State 3: Freelook Completed (Green)**
  - Triggered by Manager confirmation. Shows confirmation details.
- **Right Panel (Status Dropdown)**
  - Simplified to only show `Freelook` (Under Freelook, Under Verification, Completed) and `Payout` (Pending, Received).

### 2. New Stage: Policy Cancelled
A new terminal stage "Policy Cancelled" will be introduced after "Policy Issued". 

- **Stage Navigation**: Added to the main stage bar but only reachable via the "Request Cancellation" flow (not manual drop-off).
- **Cancellation Flow Modal**: Triggered by the "Request Cancellation" button in Policy Issued. Captures cancellation reason and notes before moving the lead.
- **Center Panel (Vertical Tracker)**
  - A 4-step vertical tracker: `Cancellation Requested` → `Cancellation Confirmed` → `Refund Initiated` → `Refund Completed`.
  - Captures critical data like Refund Amount, Expected Date, and Actual Date + Source.
  - Interactive buttons to progress through steps.
- **Right Panel**: Custom status dropdowns for `Cancellation` and `Refund`.

### 3. MIS & Payouts Dashboard
A completely new page outside of the CRM lead detail view.

- **Sidebar Integration**: Added under "Manage Leads".
- **4 Tab Layout**:
  - **MIS Uploads**: Drag-and-drop upload zone, processing logic, and a history table. Features a drill-down view showing matched policies and inline "Confirm Freelook Completed" buttons.
  - **Commission Tracker**: Centralized table for all Policy Issued leads. Calculates 20% commission expected, handles Payin/Payout status toggles for the Finance team, and tracks overdue commissions.
  - **Freelook Tracker**: Table prioritizing leads by Freelook Days Remaining. Includes inline Manager actions.
  - **Payout Discrepancies**: Table highlighting mismatches between CRM data and uploaded MIS data (Premium gap, missing policies).

## Verification Plan
### Automated & Logic Checks
- Verify `BUCKETS` array logic propagates "Policy Cancelled" correctly across the table filter and tab headers.
- Verify date-math correctly calculates Freelook expiry dates for various products (15 days vs 30 days).

### Manual Verification
- Proceed with "Request Cancellation" on an active lead, verify it moves to "Policy Cancelled".
- Switch to Manager view, navigate to MIS & Payouts, simulate an MIS upload, and verify the "Confirm Freelook Completed" button becomes active on the relevant leads.
- Verify Finance inline toggles on the Commission Tracker update lead state persistently.
args.Description: Initial plan for Policy Cancelled stage and MIS dashboard

ArtifactMetadata.Summary: Walkthrough of the Policy Lifecycle and MIS dashboard

args.CodeContent:
# Policy Lifecycle & MIS Payouts Integration

I have successfully implemented all three major updates requested, fundamentally transforming how policies are tracked post-issuance and introducing a dedicated financial management suite.

## 1. Policy Issued Bucket Redesign
The old static cards have been replaced with a dynamic, system-driven **Freelook Status Tracker**.

- **Under Freelook (Amber)**: The policy automatically begins with a countdown timer showing exact days left based on the product’s configuration (e.g., 15 days for CARE Health).
- **Under Verification (Blue)**: Once the countdown hits zero, it auto-transitions to awaiting verification.
- **Freelook Completed (Green)**: Managers can confirm the freelook is over natively from the MIS dashboard.

> [!TIP]
> The "Request Cancellation" button is only available during the "Under Freelook" window. Once past freelook, it disappears to enforce process compliance.

## 2. Policy Cancelled Stage
A brand-new terminal stage added directly after "Policy Issued", reachable exclusively through the "Request Cancellation" flow.

- **4-Step Vertical Tracker**: We've implemented a step-by-step confirmation UI mapping to operations:
  1. **Requested**: Logs the agent, date, and reason.
  2. **Confirmed**: Acknowledges insurer acceptance.
  3. **Refund Initiated**: Captures required Expected Date and Amount.
  4. **Refund Completed**: Finalizes the flow with Actual Date and Confirmation Source.

## 3. MIS & Payouts Dashboard
This is a brand new page accessible from the left sidebar navigation under "Manage Leads".

- **MIS Uploads**: Features a simulation to upload insurer files, instantly parsing and mapping them to CRM records.
- **Commission Tracker**: Centralized ledger showing expected commission (calculated automatically as 20% of premium). The Finance team can inline-toggle Payin/Payout statuses between Pending and Received without needing to open individual leads.
- **Freelook Tracker**: A prioritized list sorting leads by the fewest freelook days remaining. Managers can one-click confirm freelook completions right from this table.
- **Payout Discrepancies**: Automatically surfaces gaps, like a ₹450 mismatch between the CRM premium and the insurer's uploaded MIS data, helping operations quickly plug revenue leaks.

> [!NOTE]
> You can try the entire flow by navigating to a "Policy Issued" lead, testing the "Request Cancellation" flow, or visiting the new "MIS & Payouts" page in the sidebar.
args.Description: Update walkthrough with details on the new MIS page and Cancelled stage

ArtifactMetadata.Summary: Python script to fix syntax errors

args.CodeContent:
import sys

with open('/Users/tusharagarwal/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/sangam-crm/index.html', 'r', encoding='utf8') as f:
    html = f.read()

# Replace all \` with `
# But we need to be careful not to replace it everywhere if not intended.
# The issue only exists from window.globalMISData = []; to // Add call to renderMISPage inside navigateNav if needed

start_marker = "window.globalMISData = [];"
end_marker = "function renderManagerDashboard() {"

start_idx = html.find(start_marker)
end_idx = html.find(end_marker)

if start_idx != -1 and end_idx != -1:
    section = html[start_idx:end_idx]
    
    # Python's raw string interpretation for `\`` and `\${` in the file.
    # The file currently has literal backslash followed by backtick and literal backslash followed by ${
    section = section.replace('\\`', '`')
    section = section.replace('\\${', '${')
    
    html = html[:start_idx] + section + html[end_idx:]
    
    with open('/Users/tusharagarwal/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/sangam-crm/index.html', 'w', encoding='utf8') as f:
        f.write(html)
    print("Fixed syntax errors successfully.")
else:
    print("Error: Could not find block boundaries.")
args.Description: Python script to fix JS syntax errors caused by python escaping

ArtifactMetadata.Summary: Implementation Plan for PolicyBazaar iframe-based Quote Generation Flow

args.CodeContent:
# End-to-End PolicyBazaar iframe Prototype

This plan details the replacement of the screenshot + OCR flow with a full iframe-based quote generation prototype for Property Insurance, Credit Life, and Term Plan.

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Pre-qual Form Updates (New / Contacted Stage)
- **Button Rename & Handler:** Modify the logic in `renderProductTabsForm` so that when `activeTab` is a PB product, the button text changes from "Open PolicyBazaar →" to "Generate Quote →". The `onclick` handler will be changed to a new function `openPBPrototypeFlow()` instead of the old `openPolicyBazaarFlow()`.
- **State Transition:** `openPBPrototypeFlow()` will transition the lead to `Contacted`, set status to `Interested / Need Quote`, and initialize a new `window.pbQuoteFlowState = 'iframe'`.

### 2. Mocking PB Quote Generation (Contacted Stage)
- **UI Rewrite in `renderContactedStage`:** When `window.pbQuoteFlowState` is evaluated, the old logic (upload UI, parsing, etc.) will be replaced.
- **Iframe State (`'iframe'`):**
  - **Summary Strip:** Display a read-only `#F0EEFB` strip summarizing the pre-qual data (City, Property Type, Market Value etc.) dynamically derived from `currentLead.bucketData`. Include an "Edit Details" link to collapse the iframe and return to the form.
  - **Iframe Container:** A 560px tall, 100% width iframe using a mock data URL string (`data:text/html,...`) to render the PB interface with filter chips and quotes.
  - **CRM Overlay Strip:** Fixed at the bottom containing instructions on the left and the "Extract Quotes →" button on the right.
- **Extracting State (`'extracting'`):**
  - On clicking "Extract Quotes", set state to `'extracting'` and show the 1.5s loader overlay on the iframe.
- **Extracted State (`'extracted'`):**
  - The iframe height transitions to 0px.
  - A collapsed "PB workspace" bar appears containing "Re-open →" logic.
  - The mocked quote cards (3 for Property, 3 for Credit Life, 3 for Term) slide into view. These cards will reuse the existing Loan Protection quote card UI (with product-specific accent colors and data chips).
  - Checkboxes and the existing `shareQuote()` function will work seamlessly with these mocked cards, preserving the standard "Share Selected Quotes" CRM logic.

### 3. Payment Flow Updates (Quote Stage)
- **Contextual Prompt & Re-open PB:** In `renderQuoteStage`, when an agent is on a PB product and needs a payment link, we'll append UI below the "Paste Payment Link" input:
  - "Get this link from PolicyBazaar. Re-open the workspace..."
  - A "Re-open PolicyBazaar →" button.
- **Mock Payment Flow:**
  - Clicking "Re-open" will show a dummy iframe at full height showing a mock "Proceed to Payment" page.
  - A CRM overlay at the bottom will provide a "Copy Link" button that copies a dummy URL (e.g., `https://policybazaar.com/pay/INS49202-PLAN-TOKEN`).
  - Once the agent pastes this link, the rest of the LP payment verification flow proceeds unaltered.

### 4. Mock Data Configuration
- **Property Insurance**: Bajaj General (Bharat Griha Raksha), HDFC Ergo (My Home Long Term), SBI General (Griha Raksha Plus).
- **Credit Life**: HDFC Life (Loan Secure), TATA AIA (Saral Jeevan), Max Life (Smart Secure).
- **Term Plan**: ICICI Prudential (iProtect Smart), HDFC Life (Click 2 Protect), Bajaj Life (eTouch).
- **Banner:** A small prototype disclaimer banner will be added above the iframe area.

## Verification Plan

### Manual Verification
- Generate Quote from a PB product in the New bucket to verify the iframe and summary strip render correctly.
- Click "Extract Quotes" to verify the 1.5s loading animation and correct mock quote population based on product type.
- Share a quote, navigate to Quote bucket, and verify the "Re-open PolicyBazaar" payment flow displays the payment iframe correctly.
- Ensure the pre-qual edit link correctly returns the user to the form.
args.Description: Implementation Plan for the iframe PB prototype

# Task Completion Walkthrough

I have successfully recovered from the earlier environment reset and fully implemented all of your requested features for the Sangam CRM!

Here is a summary of the completed tasks and how you can verify them:

## 1. Iframe-based Quote Generation Flow
The screenshot/OCR flow has been replaced with an interactive iframe-based prototype for the PB Quote flow. 
- **Verify:** Open a lead in the `Contacted` stage and fill out the details. Click the **Generate Quote →** button (this button now appears consistently). The Pre-qual form will cleanly collapse into a summary strip at the top, and the mock PB iframe will open below.
- Clicking **Extract Quotes** will simulate extraction and render the CRM quote cards.

## 2. Policy Details Extraction
- **Verify:** Progress a lead to the `Policy Issued` stage. You will now see the newly added fields for Policy Extraction: `Plan Name` (prefilled), `Policy Number`, `Policy Start Date`, `Policy End Date`, `Premium` (prefilled), and `Loan Account Number`.

## 3. UI Refinements
- The text "Transaction ID" has been updated to "Payment ID".

## 4. Policy Issued Bucket Redesign & Cancelled Stage
- The **Policy Issued** center panel has been completely overhauled with the new structure.
- The `Freelook Period` tracker and `MIS & Payout Tracking` cards are fully dynamic.
- **Verify:** The new `Policy Cancelled` state is now available in the status dropdown and renders a new center panel with a 4-step cancellation tracker.

## 5. MIS & Payouts Section
- The MIS & Payouts section has been completely detached from the individual lead view into its own dedicated dashboard page.
- **Verify:** Click **MIS & Payouts** in the left navigation menu to view the global trackers for MIS Uploads, Commission Tracking, Freelook Tracking, and Payout Discrepancies. 

All systems are fully functional and ready for testing!

ArtifactMetadata.Summary: Implementation plan for MIS-driven policy confirmation and PB Iframe workflow

args.CodeContent:
# Implementation Plan: MIS-Driven Policy Confirmation

This document outlines the architectural changes required to shift from manual policy copy uploads to an automated, MIS-driven confirmation flow.

## Open Questions
- **Action for Unmatched Leads:** When a manager clicks "Find Lead →" for an unmatched MIS row, should we implement a full search modal right now, or a simple `prompt()` to enter a Lead ID for linking?
- **Data persistence:** We will simulate the backend by using Javascript objects in memory for the MIS Upload History and processed rows. Is this acceptable for the prototype?

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Refactor Milestones and Payment Done Stage
#### [MODIFY] `index.html`
- **Global Config:** Introduce `MILESTONE_CONFIG` globally to replace hardcoded milestone arrays.
- **Milestone Rendering:** Rewrite `renderPaymentDoneStage` to map the current lead to a `MILESTONE_CONFIG` key. It will dynamically render the milestones.
- **Awaiting Policy Copy Card:** In `renderPaymentDoneStage`, if the lead has completed all milestones in the config, append the "Awaiting Policy Copy Confirmation" card at the bottom, removing any existing "Upload Policy Copy" sections.

### 2. MIS Upload Tab & Processing Logic
#### [MODIFY] `index.html` (MIS Section)
- **Upload Form:** Update `renderMISUploadsTab` to include the Insurer/Source dropdown (`PolicyBazaar (Daily)`, `CARE Health (Monthly)`, `TATA AIG (Monthly)`), the "MIS Date" picker, the file upload area, and the "Process MIS" button.
- **Processing Logic (`processGlobalMIS`):**
  - Implement a mock parser that simulates reading Excel/CSV data.
  - **Step 1:** Filter for `utm_source === 'ambak'`.
  - **Step 2:** Match against `LEADS` using `policy_number`, or a combination of `name`, `insurer`, `premium`, and `start_date`.
  - **Step 3:** Assign action flags based on `laststatus` (`Sale Complete`, `Soft Copy Received`, `Refund Completed`).
- **Results Table:** Render the processing results table showing Matched/Unmatched status, and Action buttons ("Confirm Policy Copy Received", "Mark as Freelook Cancelled", "Find Lead →").
- **Bulk Actions:** Add a floating bulk action bar that appears when multiple checkboxes are selected.

### 3. Policy Confirmation Actions
#### [MODIFY] `index.html` (Global Handlers)
- **`confirmPolicyCopyReceived(leadId, misRow)`:** 
  - Move lead from `Payment Done` to `Policy Issued`.
  - Set `freelookState = 'under_freelook'`.
  - Auto-populate policy details on the lead object.
  - Add activity log and show toast.
- **`markFreelookCancelled(leadId)`:**
  - Move lead to `Policy Cancelled`.
  - Pre-fill cancellation reason.
  - Set cancellation step to "Refund Initiated".

### 4. MIS History Table
#### [MODIFY] `index.html`
- Below the results table, render the historical uploads from `window.globalMISData`.
- Include columns: Upload Date, Source, MIS Date, File Name, Total Rows, Ambak Rows, Matched, Unmatched, Confirmed, Status.

### 5. Policy Issued Redesign (Read-only)
#### [MODIFY] `index.html` (renderPolicyIssuedStage)
- Convert "Policy Number", "Insurer Name", "Plan Name", "Premium Amount", "Policy Start Date", "Policy End Date", and "Loan Account Number" to read-only text displays.
- Append a small `[MIS]` badge (background `#EEF0FD`, color `#473391`) next to each label to indicate the data source.
- Auto-calculate Freelook Expiry Date using `FREELOOK_DAYS`.
- Remove the "Save Policy Details" button.

## Verification Plan
### Manual Verification
1. Progress a lead through the milestone tracker until all steps are complete; verify the "Awaiting Policy Copy Confirmation" card appears.
2. Navigate to MIS & Payouts, select "PolicyBazaar (Daily)" and upload a mock file. Verify the results table appears with matched/unmatched rows.
3. Click "Confirm Policy Copy Received" on a matched row. Verify the lead automatically moves to `Policy Issued` with all data prefilled and marked with `[MIS]` badges.
4. Verify the bulk action bar functions properly when selecting multiple rows.
args.Description: Plan for MIS-driven policy confirmation

  - Move lead from `Payment Done` to `Policy Issued`.
  - Set `freelookState = 'under_freelook'`.
  - Auto-populate policy details on the lead object.
  - Add activity log and show toast.
- **`markFreelookCancelled(leadId)`:**
  - Move lead to `Policy Cancelled`.
  - Pre-fill cancellation reason.
  - Set cancellation step to "Refund Initiated".

### 4. MIS History Table
#### [MODIFY] `index.html`
- Below the results table, render the historical uploads from `window.globalMISData`.
- Include columns: Upload Date, Source, MIS Date, File Name, Total Rows, Ambak Rows, Matched, Unmatched, Confirmed, Status.

### 5. Policy Issued Redesign (Read-only)
#### [MODIFY] `index.html` (renderPolicyIssuedStage)
- Convert "Policy Number", "Insurer Name", "Plan Name", "Premium Amount", "Policy Start Date", "Policy End Date", and "Loan Account Number" to read-only text displays.
- Append a small `[MIS]` badge (background `#EEF0FD`, color `#473391`) next to each label to indicate the data source.
- Auto-calculate Freelook Expiry Date using `FREELOOK_DAYS`.
- Remove the "Save Policy Details" button.

## Verification Plan
### Manual Verification
1. Progress a lead through the milestone tracker until all steps are complete; verify the "Awaiting Policy Copy Confirmation" card appears.
2. Navigate to MIS & Payouts, select "PolicyBazaar (Daily)" and upload a mock file. Verify the results table appears with matched/unmatched rows.
3. Click "Confirm Policy Copy Received" on a matched row. Verify the lead automatically moves to `Policy Issued` with all data prefilled and marked with `[MIS]` badges.
4. Verify the bulk action bar functions properly when selecting multiple rows.

ArtifactMetadata.Summary: Update walkthrough to document MIS-driven confirmation flow.

args.CodeContent:
# Feature Walkthrough: MIS-Driven Policy Confirmation

I have completed the transition from manual policy uploads to an automated, MIS-driven flow as requested. This significantly improves accuracy and reduces manual work for your agents.

## 1. MIS Upload Tab & Automated Processing
The MIS Uploads tab has been completely rebuilt to act as the primary confirmation mechanism for policy issuances and cancellations.
- **Upload interface:** Easily select the source (e.g., `PolicyBazaar (Daily)`), the reporting date, and upload the `.xlsx/.csv` file. 
- **Processing Engine:** When you click "Process MIS", the system automatically:
  1. Filters for `utm_source = 'ambak'`.
  2. Matches records against Sangam leads.
  3. Maps MIS status logic (`Sale Complete`, `Soft Copy Received`, `Refund Completed`) into actionable CRM events.
- **Results Table:** Shows a clean breakdown of Matched vs. Unmatched records. Unmatched rows allow you to quickly search for a lead, while Matched rows provide 1-click confirmation buttons.
- **Bulk Action Bar:** Check multiple rows at once to trigger bulk confirmations or cancellations.

## 2. Redesigned Payment Done & Milestones
- **Centralized `MILESTONE_CONFIG`**: Milestone tracking is now driven by a central, maintainable configuration for all 5 insurance products. The `policy_copy_upload` node has been completely removed.
- **"Awaiting Policy Copy Confirmation" Card**: Once a lead completes all product-specific milestones, a new "waiting" state card appears, informing the agent that confirmation will happen automatically via MIS upload.

## 3. Read-Only Policy Issued Stage
- The Policy Issued stage is now entirely populated by the data coming from the MIS upload. 
- All fields (Plan, Policy Number, Policy Start Date, Premium, etc.) are set to **read-only** and tagged with an `[MIS]` badge to denote data provenance.
- The **Freelook Expiry Date** is automatically calculated and displayed dynamically based on the Policy Start Date provided by the MIS.

## 4. One-Click Workflows
- **Confirm Policy Copy Received:** Clicking this on a matched MIS row instantly updates the lead's status, populates all policy details, changes the bucket to `Policy Issued`, sets the freelook status to `under_freelook`, and adds a detailed audit log entry.
- **Mark as Freelook Cancelled:** Clicking this on a matched refund row instantly moves the lead to the `Policy Cancelled` bucket at the `Refund Initiated` step, pre-filling the cancellation reason.

## 5. MIS History Tracking
- A persistent MIS Upload History table is maintained below the processing results, tracking the date, file name, matched/unmatched records, and status of every upload.

You can verify all of this by completing a lead's milestones in the `Payment Done` stage, navigating to `MIS & Payouts`, processing a mock upload, and observing the automated workflow!

args.CodeContent:
# Implementation Plan: QUOTE Bucket Redesign

This document outlines the architectural changes required to implement the new QUOTE bucket design, including the dual-path center panel and dynamic right panel updates.

## User Review Required
Please review the proposed structure. Once you approve, I will begin execution and track progress.

## Open Questions
- **Pre-fill Data**: In the LP Calculator, the prompt mentions the Loan Amount is pre-filled from the HL record. I will use `currentLead.si` and `currentLead.loanBank` for this, and fallback to dummy data if missing.
- **Product Switching**: When "Change Product" is clicked, we will swap Section 2 with the product selection grid from `renderProductSelection()`. Is that correct?
- **Extract Quotes API**: I will use a simulated `setTimeout` function for the "Extract Quotes with AI" button, showing the mock list after 2 seconds. Let me know if you prefer a different mock approach.

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Update `renderQuoteStage()` (Center Panel)
- **Section 1: Change Product Bar**: 
  - Add a top bar showing the selected product and a "Change Product" button.
  - Implement a state variable (`currentLead.isChangingProduct`) to swap Section 2 with the product selection grid.
- **Section 2: Quote Generation Area**:
  - **Path A (LP)**: Build the in-house calculator card with Loan Amount, Tenure, Plan Type, and Add-ons. Add the "Calculate Premiums" button that reveals two mock quote cards (CARE and TATA AIG) with share options.
  - **Path B (Property / CL / Term)**: Build the 2-step card. Step 1 is the Pre-qualification form (dynamically switching fields based on product) with an "Open PolicyBazaar" button. Step 2 is the screenshot upload area, an "Extract Quotes with AI" button, and the resulting list of selectable extracted quotes.
  - Implement the **Share action logic** (toasts, auto-updating right panel, and logging to Activity History).

### 2. Update `STATE_MACHINE_CONFIG`
Modify the `Quote` bucket config in the `STATE_MACHINE_CONFIG` object:
- **Quote Shared**: `Consent Pending`, `Exploring Options`, `Visit Requested`
- **Payment Link Shared**: `Awaiting Payment`, `Link Not Working`, `Resend Requested`
- **Payment Done**: `Verification Pending`, `Verification Failed`, `Verified`

### 3. Update `renderRightPanel()` and Handlers
- Update the layout to dynamically hide Follow-up Date and Pending With fields when Status is "Payment Done".
- Inject the **Payment Verification** section (Amount, UTR, Date, Upload Proof) when Status is "Payment Done".
- Modify the `handleDetailStatusChange()` and `saveDetailStatusUpdate()` functions to validate all 4 verification fields before enabling the "Update Status" button.

## Verification Plan
### Manual Verification
- Navigate to the Quote bucket for an LP lead and verify the calculator and share flows.
- Navigate to the Quote bucket for a Term lead and verify the 2-step PolicyBazaar flow and quote extraction.
- Change the status in the right panel to "Payment Done" and verify the mandatory payment verification fields appear and validate properly.

args.CodeContent:
# Walkthrough: Quote Bucket Implementation

I have successfully replaced the old Quote bucket layout with the new design specified in your prompt. 

## What was changed

### 1. The Quote Bucket Center Panel
The new center panel logic has been cleanly structured into two sections within the `renderQuoteStage` function:
- **Section 1 (Change Product Bar)**: A sticky bar at the top displaying the currently selected product with a "Change Product" button. When clicked, it swaps Section 2 to the product selection grid from the Contacted bucket, and changes the button to "Confirm Product".
- **Section 2 (Quote Generation)**:
  - **Path A (Loan Protection)**: Features a robust in-house calculator card displaying the Pre-filled Loan Amount and Tenure. After clicking "Calculate Premiums", the CARE Health and TATA AIG quote cards appear below, complete with "Share All", WhatsApp, and Email buttons.
  - **Path B (Property / Credit Life / Term Plan)**: Features the 2-step PolicyBazaar flow. Step 1 shows the dynamic Pre-qualification form (which renders different fields depending on the product) and the "Open PolicyBazaar Workspace" button. Step 2 shows the yellow PB banner, the drag-and-drop screenshot upload area, and the "Extract Quotes with AI" button.
- **Quote Extraction**: I built a mock `triggerQuoteExtraction` function that simulates a 2-second extraction delay using a loading spinner, then renders a list of mock extracted quotes with checkboxes and inline "Edit" links.

### 2. The Quote Bucket Right Panel
- **Status & Sub Status Config**: I updated `STATE_MACHINE_CONFIG` for the Quote bucket, injecting your required states:
  - `Quote Shared` (Consent Pending, Exploring Options, Visit Requested)
  - `Payment Link Shared` (Awaiting Payment, Link Not Working, Resend Requested)
  - `Payment Done` (Verification Pending, Verification Failed, Verified)
- **Payment Done Verification Logic**: When the agent selects "Payment Done", the right panel dynamically hides the Follow-up Date and Pending With fields, and reveals the **PAYMENT VERIFICATION** fields (Amount, UTR, Date, Upload Proof). 
- **Validation**: I added the `validatePaymentFields` javascript function to keep the "Update Status" button disabled until all 4 verification fields are filled out.

### 3. Share Activity Flow
- Clicking any "Share" button (WhatsApp, Email, or Share All) now fires the global `shareQuotes` function.
- This function flashes a green toast notification ("Quote shared successfully").
- It automatically updates the lead's status to "Quote Shared" and sub-status to "Consent Pending".
- It pushes a new entry to the Activity History feed detailing the share event.

## Validation Results
- Verified that there are no Javascript syntax errors by programmatically checking the AST / brace parity of `index.html`.
- The UI handles the complex `isChangingProduct` state cleanly without breaking out of the Quote bucket.
- The `isQuoteExtracting` state correctly shows the spinner inside the extract button.

args.CodeContent:
# Implementation Plan: New Bucket & Layout Restructure

This document outlines the architectural changes required to implement the NEW bucket UI, remove the left sidebar, and update the state machine.

## User Review Required
> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Discrepancy Noted:** Your prompt text requested moving the 3 tabs (Activity History, Follow Up, Documents) to the **Center Panel** below the stage bar. However, the screenshot you just provided clearly shows those 3 tabs at the bottom of the **Right Panel**, matching the Quote bucket layout we just built. 
> 
> **Decision:** To keep the UI consistent and match your screenshot, **I will keep the 3 tabs in the Right Panel**. I will only implement the center panel empty state, remove the left sidebar, and add the "Lead Details →" link. Please approve if this is correct!

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Global Layout Updates (Removing Left Sidebar)
- Modify the overall `index.html` structure to remove the `detail-left` sidebar div.
- Update the layout flexbox to ensure it's just a 2-panel layout: a fluid Center Panel and a fixed 300px Right Panel.
- Modify the Header (Row 1): Insert a `Lead Details →` link (colored `#473391` with font-size `10px`) right next to the Insurance ID block. 

### 2. Update `STATE_MACHINE_CONFIG` for 'New' Bucket
Update the 'New' bucket statuses to perfectly match the prompt requirements:
- **Not Connected**: Customer Busy, Not Reachable, Switched Off, Ringing No Answer, Voicemail, Customer Disconnected Call. (Mandatory Follow Up)
- **Call Back**: Busy, Call Later, Call Later - Partial Discussion, Customer Disconnected Call, Language Barrier. (Mandatory Follow Up)
- **Interested**: Need Quote. (No Follow Up)
- **Not Interested**: Too Expensive, Already Insured, Not Required, Will Purchase Later, Family Not Interested, Other. (No Follow Up)

### 3. Center Panel (`renderCenterPanel`)
- Ensure the Stage Progress bar perfectly matches the chevron styling described in the prompt (Purple for active, Light Purple for completed, White/Gray for future).
- For the `New` bucket content area: render the simple centered empty state text: `"Call the customer and record the outcome in Update Status."`

### 4. Right Panel (`renderRightPanel` & Handlers)
- Keep the existing Right Panel structure we built for the Quote stage, but ensure the title is "Update Status".
- Update `handleDetailStatusChange` so that **FOLLOW-UP DATE** and **PENDING WITH** are completely hidden when the status is "Interested" or "Not Interested" (since follow-ups aren't required).
- Insert the static sample Activity History entry into the History tab content (System Update by Verification Agent).

## Verification Plan
### Manual Verification
- Verify the left sidebar is gone and the center panel expands to fill the space.
- Navigate to a "New" lead and verify the right panel status dropdowns default correctly.
- Select "Interested" and verify the Follow-up and Pending With fields disappear.
- Select "Not Connected" and verify they reappear.

`; } if (tab === 'Documents') { return `
