ArtifactMetadata.Summary: Implementation plan for the Payment Link Flow in Quote Bucket

args.CodeContent:
# Implement Payment Link Flow (Quote Bucket)

This plan covers the implementation of the customer plan selection and the payment link sharing flow in the Quote Bucket.

## User Review Required

> [!IMPORTANT]
> The prompt mentions: *"After quotes are shared, a new section appears below the extracted quote cards titled 'Customer's Selected Plan' ... Each quote card from the extracted list now has an additional 'Mark as Selected' button"*. 
> 
> To clarify: Should the "Mark as Selected" buttons appear directly on the **existing** quote cards that were just generated/extracted? If so, I will place the "Customer's Selected Plan" title directly above those existing quote cards (once they have been shared), rather than duplicating the quote cards in a new section below. Please confirm if this is the correct interpretation.

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Header Updates (`index.html`)
- Modify `renderMetaStrip()` to check for `currentLead.lockedPlan`.
- If a plan is locked, update the product pill from `Draft` (gray) to `Locked` (green).
- Update the Insurer line to display the dynamically selected insurer, and remove the `[Change]` link completely.

### 2. Quote Cards Update (`index.html`)
- Update the LP (Path A) hardcoded quote cards and the PB (Path B) `extCard` generator.
- Inject the **"Mark as Selected"** button into Row 2 (the action row) next to the Edit link.
- Implement the selected state:
  - If a card is marked as selected, inject the green `✓ Customer Selected Plan` banner at the top of that specific card.
  - Apply `opacity: 0.5` and `pointer-events: none` to all other unselected cards in the grid/list.
  - Render a `Change Selection` link below the locked card to allow resetting the state.

### 3. Payment Link Card (`index.html`)
- Create a new `renderPaymentLinkCard()` function.
- This card will conditionally render immediately below the quote cards *only* when a plan is locked.
- Implement the "Paste Payment Link" input field with dynamic helper text (CARE/TATA vs PolicyBazaar).
- Implement the "Send via WhatsApp" and "Send via Email" buttons (disabled until input is populated).
- Add the success state: green toast notification, input field locking (read-only with green tick), and a `Resend` link.

### 4. Right Panel State Machine (`index.html`)
- Update `STATE_MACHINE_CONFIG` for the `Quote` stage to include:
  - `Quote Shared` (Consent Pending, Exploring Options, Visit Requested)
  - `Payment Link Shared` (Awaiting Payment, Link Not Working, Resend Requested)
  - `Payment Done` (Verification Pending, Verification Failed, Verified)
- Modify `handleDetailStatusChange` to dynamically show/hide the Payment Verification inputs (Amount, UTR, Date, Proof) when `Payment Done` is selected.

### 5. Interaction Handlers (`index.html`)
- `lockPlan(insurer, planName, premium)`: Sets `currentLead.lockedPlan = true`, updates `finalInsurer`, and pushes an Activity Log.
- `unlockPlan()`: Reverts the lock state.
- `sharePaymentLink(method)`: Pushes the "Payment link shared" Activity Log, sets `status` to "Payment Link Shared", and auto-updates the Right Panel.

## Verification Plan
### Manual Verification
- Generate LP quotes and verify that clicking "Mark as Selected" locks the card and dims the other.
- Verify the header instantly updates to a green "Locked" pill.
- Ensure the Payment Link box appears below, accepts text, and enables the Share buttons.
- Confirm that sharing updates the right panel dropdowns seamlessly and injects the Activity logs.
args.Description: Create implementation plan

ArtifactMetadata.Summary: Walkthrough for Payment Link Flow

args.CodeContent:
# Walkthrough: Payment Link Flow

The Payment Link flow has been successfully built and integrated into the Quote bucket!

## What was implemented

### 1. Customer Plan Selection
- The extracted quote cards and the LP generated quote cards now feature a **"Mark as Selected"** button.
- Clicking this locks the plan:
  - The selected card elevates visually and receives the `✓ Customer Selected Plan` green banner.
  - All other unselected quotes instantly dim and become non-interactable.
  - The top compact header visually locks the product (the draft tag changes to a green `Locked` tag, and the selected Insurer's name replaces the generic `PB Partner` text).

### 2. Payment Link Card
- As soon as a plan is locked, a dedicated Payment Link section renders directly below the quotes.
- It features a clean summary strip of the locked plan at the top, along with a "Paste Payment Link" input field.
- The WhatsApp and Email share buttons remain disabled until the agent pastes a link into the input. 

### 3. State Engine & Sharing
- Hitting one of the share buttons successfully pushes the payment link to the customer and throws a success toast.
- The Payment Link input becomes read-only and locks itself with a green tick.
- The right-side status panel **automatically updates** to `Payment Link Shared`, setting the sub-status to `Awaiting Payment`.
- The Follow-up Date constraints enforce correctly, and a new descriptive entry drops into the Activity History tab!

Refresh your browser and test out locking a plan and generating a payment link. Let me know when you're ready to proceed to the next flow!
args.Description: Create walkthrough

${amt} · ${bank}
${stage}
`; } else { loanHtml = `
—
`; } let partnerName = 'E2E Sales'; // Mock let rmName = 'Ambak BM'; // Mock html += `
${l.id}

${l.source || 'Manual'}

${rmName}
${nextFup}
${createdDateStr}
${leadAge}
`; }); if(tbody) tbody.innerHTML = html;""" html = pattern.sub(new_render_code.replace('\\', '\\\\'), html) with open('/Users/tusharagarwal/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/sangam-crm/index.html', 'w', encoding='utf8') as f: f.write(html)

# Implement Bulk Assign Flow

This plan outlines the architecture for introducing bulk reassignment capabilities to the "My Insurance Leads" table for IBM Managers.

## Proposed Changes

### 1. State Management
I will introduce new global variables to track bulk assign state:
- `window.bulkAssignMode = false;`
- `window.selectedBulkLeads = new Set();`

### 2. Header and Banner UI
- Inject the **"Bulk Assign"** button right next to "Bulk Upload" (hidden via CSS display logic for IBM Agents).
- Introduce a dynamic blue action banner (`div#bulk-action-banner`) directly above the leads table container.
  - When `bulkAssignMode === false`, this banner is hidden (`display: none`).
  - When active, it displays the selection count.
  - An `Assign X Leads →` button will render if `selectedBulkLeads.size > 0`.
  - A `Cancel` button will allow the manager to exit the mode.

### 3. Table UI Enhancements
- Modify `renderLeadTable` to conditionally inject an additional `<th>` and `<td>` for the checkbox column based on `bulkAssignMode`.
- **Select All Logic:** The header checkbox will evaluate whether `selectedBulkLeads.size === LEADS.length`. If true, it deselects all. Otherwise, it populates `selectedBulkLeads` with all visible lead IDs. (Note: Only visible/filtered leads will be selected as requested).
- **Row Styling:** If a lead ID is in `selectedBulkLeads`:
  - Background color applied `#F0EEFB`
  - Left border `3px solid #473391` (via inline style or dynamically toggled class)
- **Click Hijacking:** Modify the existing `<tr onclick="openLead(...)">`. When `bulkAssignMode` is active, clicking the row will toggle the checkbox instead of navigating to the lead detail page.

### 4. Reassignment Modal
- Add a new hidden modal `div#bulk-assign-modal` to the DOM.
- **Dynamic Content:** It will render the exact count of selected leads. It will also loop through `selectedBulkLeads` to display up to 3 lead ID pills, appending a `+X more` text string if the count exceeds 3.
- **Dropdown:** A custom styled select field will list the available IBM Agents (e.g., Rohit M, Priya S, etc.).
- **Confirm Logic:** The Confirm button will remain disabled until an agent is selected.

### 5. Execution Workflow (`confirmBulkAssign`)
When confirmed:
1. The modal closes immediately.
2. A temporary spinner overlay is activated over the table container for 1 second.
3. The underlying `LEADS` array is updated: changing the assignee for all selected IDs and injecting a "System Update" activity log into their history.
4. The table re-renders (`renderLeadTable()`).
5. A custom success toast appears for 4 seconds.
6. `bulkAssignMode` is reset to false, hiding checkboxes and the banner.

## Verification Plan
### Manual Verification
1. Click Bulk Assign and verify checkboxes and the action banner appear.
2. Filter the table (e.g., stage "Quote"), click Select All, and verify only those filtered leads are selected.
3. Select leads manually by clicking anywhere on the rows.
4. Launch the modal, assign an agent, and click confirm.
5. Verify the table loading overlay, success toast, and immediate column updates.
args.Description: Create implementation plan

args.CodeContent:
# Walkthrough: Bulk Assign Flow

The IBM Manager's Bulk Reassignment feature has been successfully deployed!

## What was implemented

### 1. Seamless Mode Switching
- Hitting the new `Bulk Assign` button puts the entire table into a multi-select mode.
- A dynamic blue action banner cascades down above the table, cleanly displaying your current selection count.
- Regular navigation clicks on the table rows are temporarily hijacked—clicking anywhere on a row will now elegantly toggle its selection instead of booting you into the lead detail view.

### 2. Multi-Select Magic
- A dedicated checkbox column slides into the left edge of the table. 
- You can individually select leads or hit the "Select All" checkbox in the header. 
- *Pro-tip:* The "Select All" checkbox respects your active filters! You can filter down to "Contacted", hit Select All, and it will only grab the filtered, visible leads.
- Selected rows light up with a subtle purple background and a sharp 3px left border.

### 3. The Assignment Modal
- Once you've selected at least one lead, the `Assign X Leads →` button reveals itself in the blue banner.
- Clicking it opens the crisp assignment modal, summarizing the IDs of the leads you are about to reassign.
- A dropdown lets you pick the destination IBM Agent. The Confirm button remains securely disabled until an agent is picked.

### 4. Seamless Data Execution
- Hitting confirm instantly throws a 1-second loading spinner over the table to simulate backend communication.
- The table silently updates the `Assign To` column for every selected lead.
- A green success toast drops in from the top.
- The system automatically exits Bulk Assign mode, returning the table back to normal.
- We even inject a new `System Update` entry into the Activity Log of every reassigned lead, stating exactly who moved it and when!

Test it out! Feel free to filter down your leads and bulk-move them. Let me know when you're ready for the next prompt.
args.Description: Create walkthrough

ArtifactMetadata.Summary: Implementation plan for New bucket redesign

args.CodeContent:
# Implement NEW Bucket Form Flow

This plan details the massive redesign of the "NEW" bucket center panel. We are replacing the empty placeholder with a rich, responsive, auto-saving data capture form featuring 4 distinct product tabs and HL (Home Loan) integration.

## Architecture & State Management

1. **State Injection:**
   - I will introduce a global state `window.newStageActiveTab` to track the currently selected tab. By default, it will infer the recommended product from `currentLead.initialProduct` (e.g., if it's "Loan Protection", that tab opens by default).
   - I will introduce a `window.newStageFormData` object to store form values dynamically as the agent types. This prevents data loss when switching tabs.
   - An auto-save function `autoSaveNewStage()` will trigger on tab switch and on input blur, simulating backend syncing and displaying the "✓ Saved" micro-interaction.

## UI Implementation Details

### Section 1: HL Lead Indicator Banner
- Built inside `renderNewStage()`.
- Condition: `if (currentLead.hlLead || currentLead.si)`.
- If true: renders the purple `#F0EEFB` banner with the linked ID, customer name, loan amount, bank, and stage. A link to open the HL lead will be provided.
- If false: renders the yellow warning `#FFF8E1` banner explicitly stating manual data entry is required.

### Section 2: Product Tabs & Content Layout
- **Tab Bar:** 4 pills (Loan Protection, Property, Credit Life, Term Plan). The active pill uses solid `#473391`. The recommended tab gets a tiny `RECOMMENDED` badge beneath it.
- **Auto-Save Notification:** A floated absolute div in the top-right of the content area that fades from "Saving..." to "✓ Saved" via a JS timeout.
- **Completion Indicator:** A bottom-right tracker evaluating required fields (`3 of 4 fields filled` vs `✓ All required fields filled`).

### Section 3: The Forms (Tabs 1-4)
I will build distinct HTML form structures for each tab inside a switch statement.

- **HL Badges & Locking:** Fields like "Loan Amount" will check for `currentLead.si`. If present, the input gets `readonly`, a grey background, `cursor: not-allowed`, the custom `HL` badge next to the label, and a padlock icon inside the input.
- **Loan Protection (Tab 1):** Date of Birth, Loan Amount (locked), Tenure (select), Plan Type (select), and CARE Add-ons (checkboxes).
- **Property Insurance (Tab 2):** City, Property Type (select), Market Value, Loan Amount (locked).
- **Credit Life (Tab 3):** Date of Birth, Smoker Status (custom radio UI), Educational Qualification (select), Annual Income (select), Occupation Type (select), Loan Amount (locked).
- **Term Plan (Tab 4):** Identical structure to Credit Life.

## CSS and Field Styling
- **Labels:** `10px, uppercase, #9CA3AF`.
- **Inputs:** `38px height, Poppins, 1.5px border`.
- **Custom Radio/Checkbox:** I will write raw CSS for the radio circles (purple dot when active) and checkboxes to match the strict design requirements.

## Verification Plan
1. Open a lead in the `New` stage that has a linked HL lead. Verify the purple banner and locked Loan Amount fields.
2. Open a lead in the `New` stage without an HL lead. Verify the yellow warning banner and unlocked fields.
3. Switch between tabs and verify the active tab state updates.
4. Fill out fields and switch tabs to verify the `Saving... -> ✓ Saved` interaction fires.
5. Watch the Completion Indicator update dynamically as fields are filled.
args.Description: Create plan

args.CodeContent:
# Walkthrough: NEW Bucket Form Flow

The "NEW" stage bucket has been completely redesigned from an empty state into a rich, data-capture powerhouse!

## What was implemented

### 1. The HL Context Banner
- At the very top of the stage, an intelligent banner now dictates the context of the lead.
- **If a Home Loan lead is linked**: The banner glows purple, displaying the associated HL ID, customer name, loan amount, and bank, alongside a link to view the HL lead.
- **If no HL lead is linked**: A yellow warning banner alerts the agent that all data entry must be performed manually.

### 2. Intelligent Product Tabs
- Below the banner, 4 distinct pill-shaped product tabs have been built (`Loan Protection`, `Property Insurance`, `Credit Life`, `Term Plan`).
- **Smart Defaulting**: When the lead is opened, the system infers the recommended product (e.g., "Loan Protection") and defaults to that tab, planting a tiny purple `RECOMMENDED` badge beneath it.
- **Auto-Save Magic**: As agents switch tabs or type into fields, the form dynamically captures their data into state. A subtle `Saving...` indicator briefly appears in the top right corner of the form, snapping to a green `✓ Saved` before cleanly fading out.

### 3. Dynamic Form Logic & HL Locking
- We built customized 2-column grid forms tailored perfectly for each product's API requirements (e.g., PolicyBazaar vs. CARE Health).
- **HL Data Locking**: For linked leads, fields like "Loan Amount" automatically pre-fill with the known value, lock themselves with a grey background, and render a tiny padlock icon inside the input. A custom `HL` badge next to the label visually explains *why* the field is locked.
- **Custom UI Elements**: The Credit Life and Term Plan tabs feature custom-built inline radio buttons (for Smoker Status), while the Loan Protection tab features custom checkboxes for the CARE Add-ons.

### 4. Live Completion Tracker
- A live tracker sits in the bottom right corner (e.g., `3 of 4 fields filled`).
- As the agent types, clicks dropdowns, or toggles radio buttons, the tracker evaluates the form in real-time. Once all fields are satisfied, it transitions to a bold green `✓ All required fields filled`.

Go ahead and test opening leads in the `New` bucket—both with and without linked loans—and try typing into the forms! Let me know when you're ready for the next prompt.

args.CodeContent:
# Cross-Bucket Structural Flow Overhaul

This plan details the structural changes requested across the `NEW`, `CONTACTED`, and `QUOTE` buckets. We are shifting the quote generation interface up the funnel into the `CONTACTED` stage, and transforming the `QUOTE` stage exclusively into a payment processing & verification bucket.

## Overview of Changes

### 1. NEW Bucket
- Add a "Generate Quote →" button at the bottom of the tab content area.
- This button will be disabled if the required fields for the active tab are not filled.
- **Action:** Clicking it transitions the lead state to `Contacted`, adds an Activity History log, and moves the UI into the `CONTACTED` bucket.

### 2. CONTACTED Bucket
Currently, the `CONTACTED` bucket displays a simple 4-card product selection UI. We will completely replace this with:
- **Form UI:** A clone of the `NEW` bucket's interactive tabs, auto-save state, and field layouts. Data populated in the `NEW` bucket will seamlessly persist here.
- **Validation:** Clicking the "Generate Quote" button inside this bucket will now trigger inline field validations (`"This field is required"` in red) instead of a hard block.
- **Quote Results (Section 2):** 
  - Once validated, quote generation occurs. 
  - For **Loan Protection (LP)**: 2 generated quote cards (CARE Health & TATA AIG) will appear below the tabs with a "Share All Quotes" button.
  - For **PB Products (PR, CL, TP)**: A pre-qual confirmation card appears summarizing the data, alongside an "Open PolicyBazaar Workspace" button which triggers the Step 2 screenshot upload flow.
- **Action:** Sharing quotes from these cards transitions the lead to the `QUOTE` bucket.

### 3. QUOTE Bucket
Currently, the `QUOTE` bucket houses both the LP calculator and PB extraction UI. This will be entirely ripped out. The `QUOTE` bucket will now only contain:
- **Section 1 (Selected Plan):** A clean summary card of the shared plan, with a "Change Plan" link that triggers a modal to select alternative quotes.
- **Section 2 (Payment Link):** The existing "Paste payment link" input and Share actions.
- **Section 3 (Payment Verification):** A brand new section always visible in this bucket.
  - Contains fields for Payment Amount, UTR Number, Payment Date, and Upload Proof.
  - Contains a role-based sub-status selector (`Verification Pending`, `Payment Failed`, `Verified`). 
  - Only IBM Managers can select `Verified`.
  - **Action:** Submitting a `Verified` status transitions the lead to the `PAYMENT DONE` bucket, locking the lead.

## Verification Plan

### Automated/Code Checks
- Verify `renderContactedStage()` accurately inherits `window.newStageFormData` and renders the correct form layout.
- Verify `renderQuoteStage()` correctly scopes out the old quote generation UI and properly renders the Payment Verification grid.

### Manual Testing
1. **Flow Test:** 
   - Open a lead in `New`. Fill required fields, click "Generate Quote".
   - Verify lead moves to `Contacted` and data is retained.
   - Click "Generate Quote" again to trigger quote results. Share a quote.
   - Verify lead moves to `Quote`. Check that the plan summary is correct and Quote Gen UI is gone.
   - Test the Payment Verification section as an IBM Agent (cannot verify) and IBM Manager (can verify).

> [!WARNING]
> This change fundamentally alters the state machine of the CRM. Previously, the `Contacted` stage was used to *select* a product, and the `Quote` stage was used to *generate* the quote. The new flow requires the user to select the product and generate the quote from the `New` or `Contacted` stage itself. I will ensure all global state objects align with this new paradigm.
args.Description: Create implementation plan

args.CodeContent:
# Walkthrough: Cross-Bucket Flow Overhaul

The structural flow of the application has been completely rewired! We have shifted the "Quote Generation" logic up into the `CONTACTED` bucket, and transformed the `QUOTE` bucket exclusively into a payment processing hub.

Here is exactly what was implemented:

### 1. The NEW Bucket
- The interactive tabs remain perfectly intact, but they now culminate in a bold **Generate Quote →** button at the bottom.
- Clicking this button automatically transitions the lead to the `CONTACTED` bucket and logs the action in the Activity History. All previously filled data persists seamlessly.

### 2. The CONTACTED Bucket (The New Generation Hub)
- The old, static 4-card product selection UI has been entirely removed.
- In its place is an exact clone of the `NEW` bucket's interactive forms.
- **Inline Validation:** Clicking "Generate Quote" here now fires inline validation logic. If any required fields are missed, they glow red.
- **Quote Results:** Once validated, the Quote Results dynamically inject *below* the tabs (the tabs remain fully accessible so you can tweak numbers!).
  - **Loan Protection:** Renders the CARE Health and TATA AIG quote cards natively with "Share All Quotes" functionality.
  - **PB Products (Property, Term, Credit Life):** Renders the clean Pre-Qual summary card, which prompts the user to open the PolicyBazaar workspace. Doing so opens the familiar screenshot upload Step 2 flow.
- Sharing quotes automatically moves the lead into the `QUOTE` bucket.

### 3. The QUOTE Bucket (Payment & Verification Hub)
- The entire Quote Generation interface has been stripped out. This bucket is strictly for closing the deal.
- **Section 1 (Selected Plan):** A crisp summary card showing exactly which plan was shared. It features a simple "Change Plan" modal link to swap insurers if the customer changes their mind.
- **Section 2 (Payment Link):** The paste-payment-link card is locked into position 2.
- **Section 3 (Payment Verification):** A brand new block!
  - It captures Payment Amount, UTR Number, Payment Date, and Upload Proof.
  - Features role-based sub-status toggles (`Verification Pending`, `Payment Failed`, `Verified`).
  - **IBM Agents** can submit the payment for verification, but the green `Verified` pill is grayed out and disabled for them.
  - **IBM Managers** can click `Verified` and confirm, which locks the lead and blasts it into the `PAYMENT DONE` bucket!

Try running a lead all the way from `New` to `Payment Done` using the new flow to see how beautifully it connects! Let me know when you're ready to tackle MIS & Payouts.

args.CodeContent:
# Walkthrough: Cross-Bucket Flow Overhaul & UI Redesign

The structural flow of the application has been completely rewired, and the core quote cards have received a massive visual upgrade! 

Here is exactly what was implemented:

### 1. The NEW & CONTACTED Bucket Forms
- The interactive tabs remain perfectly intact, but they now culminate in a bold **Generate Quote →** button at the bottom.
- Clicking this button from `NEW` automatically validates the fields behind the scenes. If everything is filled, it transitions the lead to the `CONTACTED` bucket and **auto-generates** the quotes instantly!
- **Tenure Upgrade:** The Loan Protection tab now features a sleek 5-pill toggle array for "Tenure", defaulting to 5 Years, ensuring faster data entry without an extra click.

### 2. The Redesigned Quote Cards (CONTACTED Bucket)
Once quote results are generated, the new CARE Health and TATA AIG quote cards appear below the tabs:
- **Premium Aesthetics:** We have completely abandoned the side-by-side layout in favor of a full-width, vertically stacked design.
- **Card Anatomy:** Each card features the insurer logo and plan name up top, a clean 3-column data row (Insured Value, Policy term, Add-ons), a massive bold premium display, and a dedicated bottom row detailing commission earnings and a link to the policy brochure.

### 3. The New Share Popup
- The "Share Quote" (and "Share All Quotes") buttons now trigger a beautiful, anchored popup menu instead of executing the action immediately.
- The popup elegantly presents WhatsApp and Email options, complete with colored circle icons and hover states.
- It features smart dismissal—clicking anywhere outside the popup seamlessly closes it.
- Selecting an option triggers the familiar share logic: the lead is transitioned to the `QUOTE` bucket, the status is updated to `Quote Shared`, and a green success toast appears.

### 4. The QUOTE Bucket (Payment & Verification Hub)
- The entire Quote Generation interface has been stripped out. This bucket is strictly for closing the deal.
- **Section 1 (Selected Plan):** A crisp summary card showing exactly which plan was shared.
- **Section 2 (Payment Link):** The paste-payment-link card is locked into position 2.
- **Section 3 (Payment Verification):** Captures Payment Amount, UTR Number, Payment Date, and Upload Proof. Features role-based sub-status toggles (`Verification Pending`, `Payment Failed`, `Verified`). IBM Managers can verify payments to lock the lead and blast it into the `PAYMENT DONE` bucket!
args.Description: Update walkthrough

ArtifactMetadata.Summary: Implementation plan for QUOTE bucket redesign

args.CodeContent:
# QUOTE Bucket Redesign: Payment Processing Flow

This plan details the redesign of the QUOTE bucket to unify the payment workflow into a seamless, interactive experience featuring simulated OCR extraction.

## Goal Description
The QUOTE bucket will be transformed to present all shared quotes, allow the agent to lock in the customer's choice via a "Share Payment Link" action, and then walk through a unified payment link sharing and OCR-powered verification process.

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Section 1 — Shared Quotes Re-architecture
We will display the same stacked quote cards from the `CONTACTED` bucket, but adapted for the `QUOTE` bucket:
- **Button Replacement:** The "Share Quote" buttons are replaced by a full-width "Share Payment Link" button.
- **Selection State Logic:** Clicking this button marks the plan as selected. The chosen card receives a `✓ Customer Selected` green banner, while unselected cards dim to `opacity: 0.4` and become disabled.
- **Change Selection:** A "Change Selection" link allows resetting this state.
- **Header Update:** On selection, the right-panel header pill updates to "Locked" (green) and removes the "[Change]" action. Activity history is logged.

### 2. Section 2 — Unified Payment Card
When a quote is selected, Section 2 slides down. It merges Link Sharing and Verification into one cohesive card.

**Part A: Share Payment Link**
- An input for the payment link (with dynamic helper text based on product type: CARE/TATA vs PB).
- WhatsApp and Email buttons, disabled until a link is pasted.
- On sharing, the input locks (read-only with a green check), a toast appears, and status updates to "Payment Link Shared" / "Awaiting Payment".

**Part B: Payment Verification & OCR Flow**
- Replaces the simple inputs with an interactive upload area.
- **Upload State:** On file selection, the area converts to a preview row with an "Extract Details with AI" button.
- **OCR Simulation:** Clicking extraction triggers a simulated loading state (`Extracting...`), after which 3 editable fields appear in a grid (Amount, Transaction ID, Date), complete with tiny `OCR` badges.
- **Manager Approval:** A final "Mark as Payment Verified" button appears. It validates that all 3 fields are filled. It will be visibly disabled for IBM Agents with a tooltip, but active for IBM Managers. Submitting moves the lead to `PAYMENT DONE`.

## Verification Plan

### Automated/Code Checks
- Verify JS routing and state variables for the new QUOTE UI flow (`currentLead.lockedPlan`, `currentLead.paymentLinkShared`, `currentLead.paymentOcrState`).

### Manual Testing
1. Navigate a lead into the `QUOTE` bucket.
2. Select a quote by clicking "Share Payment Link" and ensure unselected quotes dim appropriately.
3. Paste a link and share it.
4. "Upload" a file in the verification section, trigger the OCR extraction, and verify the fields appear.
5. Attempt to verify as an IBM Agent (should be disabled) and then as an IBM Manager (should succeed and move to Payment Done).

> [!WARNING]
> This redesign replaces the existing `renderQuoteStage` implementation entirely. I will need to ensure that the mocked data for the quote cards accurately reflects the specific product the lead is tied to (LP vs PB products).

args.CodeContent:
# Implementation Plan: Payment Done Bucket

This plan outlines the structural and functional changes needed to build the Payment Done bucket center panel and adjust the right panel according to the provided instructions.

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Update `renderPaymentDoneStage` Logic
We will completely rewrite `renderPaymentDoneStage()` in `index.html` to generate the three new sections.

#### Section 1: Previous Work Summary (Collapsed)
*   **State:** Default collapsed.
*   **UI:** Render a white card with a #F9FAFB header containing "Journey Summary" and a chevron toggle.
*   **Content (Expanded):**
    *   **Quotes Generated:** Compact, read-only list of generated quotes (logo, name, premium).
    *   **Quote Shared:** The locked quote details (insurer, premium, method, timestamp).
    *   **Payment:** Read-only summary of the payment block (link, verified amount, UTR, date).

#### Section 2: Milestone Tracker
*   **Dynamic Sequences:** Based on the current product (`property`, `lp` (CARE/TATA), or `tp`/`cl`), generate the specific milestone track array.
*   **Track UI:** A horizontal flow with nodes representing Done (green circle ✓), Active (purple circle), and Pending (gray circle).
*   **Active Instruction Card:** Below the track, render a #F0EEFB instruction card for the current active milestone.
    *   Title and description change dynamically based on the milestone.
*   **Action Button:** Most milestones will have a "Mark as [Next Milestone]" button.
    *   Clicking will advance the state, log activity, and re-render.
*   **Policy Decision Exception (Credit Life / Term):**
    *   Instead of one button, show 3 pills: Approved, Counter Offer, Rejected.
    *   **Counter Offer:** Expands an inline form for revised premium, changes details, and a new payment link.
    *   **Rejected:** Immediately opens the "Mark as Lost" modal pre-filled with "Insurer Rejected".

#### Section 3: Policy Copy Upload
*   **Visibility:** Only renders when the active milestone is "Policy Copy Upload".
*   **UI:** Dashed upload box for PDF/Image.
*   **Interaction:** Clicking simulates an upload, replacing the box with a file preview row.
*   **Action Button:** "Confirm & Move to Policy Issued" (disabled until upload). Moves the lead to the "Policy Issued" bucket.

### 2. Update Right Panel (`renderLeadDetail`)
*   **Conditionals:** In the `Payment Done` stage, hide the "Follow-up Date" and "Pending With" wrappers.
*   **Clean Up:** Hide any existing legacy payment verification inputs in the right panel for this stage.
*   **Statuses:** Ensure the Status and Sub Status dropdowns reflect the milestones (e.g., Status: "Tele-PD", Sub Status: "Pending").

### 3. State Management Functions
We will add/update several helper functions to the global scope:
*   `togglePaymentDoneSummary()`: To expand/collapse Section 1.
*   `advanceMilestone(milestoneName)`: To advance the tracker, update `currentLead.status`, and log activity.
*   `handlePolicyDecision(decision)`: To handle Approved/Counter Offer/Rejected flows.
*   `saveCounterOffer()`: To process the inline form submission.
*   `simulatePolicyUpload()`: To mock the file upload delay and state.
*   `moveToPolicyIssued()`: To finalize the bucket transition.

## Verification Plan

### Manual Verification
1.  Navigate a lead (e.g., Loan Protection - TATA) to the Payment Done stage.
2.  Verify **Section 1** defaults to collapsed, and when expanded, shows accurate read-only data.
3.  Verify **Section 2** shows the correct TATA AIG milestone sequence (Payment Verified -> Tele-PD Pending -> Tele-PD Completed -> Policy Copy Upload).
4.  Verify clicking "Mark as Tele-PD Completed" advances the node and updates the instruction card.
5.  Navigate a Term Plan lead to the Payment Done stage and test the **Policy Decision** multi-pill UI (especially the Counter Offer inline form).
6.  Reach the **Policy Copy Upload** milestone, simulate an upload, and successfully transition to the `Policy Issued` bucket.
7.  Check the right panel to ensure only Status, Sub Status, and Remarks are visible.
args.Description: Create implementation plan

args.CodeContent:
# Walkthrough: Payment Done Bucket

I have completely overhauled the `Payment Done` bucket to serve as a robust, product-specific milestone tracking center.

### Journey Summary (Section 1)
At the top of the bucket, you'll find a clean, collapsible **Journey Summary** card. It condenses all the critical steps from previous stages (Quotes Generated, Quote Shared, Payment Verified) into read-only rows, ensuring the agent has context without cluttering the screen.

### Processing Milestones (Section 2)
The core of this stage is the dynamic milestone tracker. It automatically adapts to the product type:
*   **Property:** KYC Pending -> KYC Completed
*   **CARE Health:** Proposal Form Pending -> Proposal Form Completed
*   **TATA AIG:** Tele-PD Pending -> Tele-PD Completed
*   **Term/Credit Life:** Documents -> Medical -> Policy Decision

The active milestone features an expanded instruction card with explicit directions and a one-click action button (e.g., "Mark as Tele-PD Completed") that instantly advances the timeline and logs the activity.

#### The Policy Decision Exception
For Term and Credit Life policies, the "Policy Decision" milestone features a specialized multi-pill workflow:
1.  **Approved:** Proceeds directly to policy issuance.
2.  **Counter Offer:** Expands an inline form to capture the revised premium, details, and an optional new payment link. Submitting this pauses the milestone for customer feedback ("Customer Accepted" / "Customer Rejected").
3.  **Rejected:** Immediately opens the "Mark as Lost" modal with pre-filled reasons and refund instructions.

### Policy Copy Upload (Section 3)
Once all intermediate milestones are cleared, the final "Policy Copy Upload" block appears. It features an interactive dashed dropzone. Clicking it simulates a successful file upload, replacing the box with a clean file preview row and unlocking the **"Confirm & Move to Policy Issued"** button.

### Right Panel Cleanup
The right panel has been stripped of unnecessary fields (Follow-up Date, Pending With) for the Payment Done stage, focusing solely on the relevant `Status` and `Sub Status` dropdowns.

args.CodeContent:
import re

with open('index.html', 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    html = f.read()

# Find all onclick attributes
onclicks = set(re.findall(r'onclick="([^"]+)"', html))

# Find all defined functions
# function foo()
func_defs = set(re.findall(r'function\s+([a-zA-Z0-9_]+)\s*\(', html))
# window.foo = function()
window_defs = set(re.findall(r'window\.([a-zA-Z0-9_]+)\s*=\s*function', html))

all_defs = func_defs.union(window_defs)

# Special cases or built-ins
built_ins = {'event.stopPropagation()', 'window.open', 'setTimeout'}

missing = []
for oc in onclicks:
    # Extract the function name being called
    # This regex looks for an identifier followed by (
    calls = re.findall(r'([a-zA-Z0-9_]+)\s*\(', oc)
    for call in calls:
        if call not in all_defs and call not in built_ins and call not in ('event', 'window', 'document', 'console'):
            missing.append(f"Missing: {call} in onclick: {oc}")

print("Missing handlers:")
for m in set(missing):
    print(m)

# Policy Issued Stage & Freelook Tracking

Implement the new `Policy Issued` stage, migrating from the old placeholder `Policy` stage. This involves updating navigation flows, adding specific UI cards for Policy Summary and Freelook Tracking, and applying configuration-driven logic.

## Proposed Changes

### Configuration Updates
- Add `FREELOOK_DAYS` constant mapping each product to its respective freelook period (CARE/TATA/Property = 15 days, Credit Life/Term Plan = 30 days).
- Update the global `BUCKETS` array to rename `Policy` to `Policy Issued`.
- Update `bucketBgMap` and `bucketColorMap` for `Policy Issued`.

### `Payment Done` Milestone Advance Action
- In `renderMilestoneTracker`, when the final action (e.g. `Policy Copy Upload`) is completed, clicking the final action button ("Mark as Policy Copy Received") will:
  - Transition the lead bucket to `Policy Issued`.
  - Add the activity log: "Agent Update · [Agent name] · Policy copy received. Lead moved to Policy Issued · Just now".
  - Show a green toast notification.

### Center Panel: `renderPolicyIssuedStage`
- Implement `renderPolicyIssuedStage()` to replace the placeholder `renderPolicyStage()`.
- **Section 1: Policy Summary (Read-Only)**
  - 2-column grid showing OCR-extracted fields (Policy Number, Insurer Name, Start Date, Sum Insured, Premium Amount).
  - Calculate `Freelook Expiry Date` dynamically based on Start Date + `FREELOOK_DAYS[product]`. Show an amber "Freelook Active" or green "Freelook Completed" pill based on the current date vs expiry date.
  - Calculate `Commission Expected` (20% of premium).
- **Section 2: Freelook Period Tracking**
  - Row of 3 toggle pills: `Pending`, `Completed`, `Policy Cancelled`.
  - Default selection to `Pending` upon entering the stage.
  - Selecting `Policy Cancelled` triggers a confirmation modal. If confirmed, lead moves to `Lost` bucket with appropriate logging and a refund note appears.
  - "Save Freelook Status" button that commits the selection to the lead object and pushes an activity log.

### Right Panel & Modal Updates
- Update `index.html` STATUS_FLOW configuration for `Policy Issued`:
  - Main status: `Freelook Period`.
  - Sub-statuses: `Pending`, `Completed`, `Policy Cancelled`.
- Remove "No Follow-up Date" or "Pending With" options in this stage.
- Update `toggleModal` logic to handle the new Policy Cancellation confirmation modal.

## Verification Plan

### Manual Verification
- Proceed through the `Payment Done` tracker and click "Mark as Policy Copy Received". Verify transition to `Policy Issued`, stage bar updates, and toast generation.
- Ensure the Center Panel displays the Policy Summary block correctly with Commission calculated and Freelook Expiry accurately determined based on the product config.
- Interact with Freelook tracking pills, confirming visual selection states (Pending/Completed/Cancelled).
- Test the Policy Cancelled path to ensure the confirmation modal appears and successfully transitions the lead to the Lost bucket.
- Verify that clicking "Save Freelook Status" accurately logs the update in the Activity History.
args.Description: Plan for Policy Issued bucket creation and freelook tracking.

args.CodeContent:
# Policy Issued & Freelook Tasks

- [ ] 1. Update Global Configs & Naming
  - [ ] Add `FREELOOK_DAYS` to `index.html`
  - [ ] Rename `Policy` to `Policy Issued` in `BUCKETS`
  - [ ] Update `bucketBgMap` and `bucketColorMap`
  - [ ] Fix instances checking `l.bucket === 'Policy'`
  
- [ ] 2. `Payment Done` to `Policy Issued` Transition
  - [ ] Update `advanceMilestone` / final upload button to change bucket to `Policy Issued`
  - [ ] Add Activity Log & Toast for the transition

- [ ] 3. Center Panel: `renderPolicyIssuedStage()`
  - [ ] Section 1: Policy Summary card (OCR fields + Freelook calculation + Commission)
  - [ ] Section 2: Freelook Period tracking (Pills: Pending, Completed, Policy Cancelled)
  - [ ] Cancellation confirmation modal HTML
  - [ ] `saveFreelookStatus` and `confirmPolicyCancellation` JS functions

- [ ] 4. Right Panel Status Config
  - [ ] Update `STATUS_FLOW` for `Policy Issued`
  - [ ] Remove Follow-up/Pending With from this specific stage

ArtifactMetadata.Summary: Implementation plan to refactor the center panel to use an accordion-based history view.

args.CodeContent:
# Accordion History View Refactor

We will transition the center panel's history visualization from the unified "Journey Summary" block into individual, per-stage collapsible accordions.

## Proposed Changes

### `index.html` (State Management & Center Panel Wrapper)
- Introduce a global `window.accordionState = {}` to track which past stages are currently expanded.
- Expose a `toggleAccordion(stageName)` function to expand/collapse these sections and trigger a re-render.
- Completely rewrite `renderCenterPanel()` to:
  - Iterate through all stages up to (but not including) the active stage.
  - Render an accordion wrapper for each past stage.
  - Render the active stage at the very bottom in its standard, fully editable form.
- Remove the old `renderJourneySummary()` function and its associated HTML calls.

### Stage Rendering Functions (Read-Only Mode)
We will modify each stage's renderer to accept a `isReadOnly` parameter (defaulting to `false`). When `isReadOnly` is `true`, the stage will render a simplified, non-interactive version of itself suitable for the expanded accordion view.

- **`renderNewStage(isReadOnly)`**:
  - Hides the top tab switcher, showing only the chosen product.
  - Converts all `<input>`, `<select>`, and radio buttons into disabled, muted versions.
  - Removes the "Generate Quote" bottom bar.
- **`renderContactedStage(isReadOnly)`**:
  - Hides the "Share Quotes" bottom action bar.
  - Renders quote cards with a "✓ Shared" badge replacing the action buttons for quotes that were shared.
- **`renderQuoteStage(isReadOnly)`**:
  - Displays the selected shared quote in a read-only state.
  - Displays the payment verification card in a read-only state, removing "Share Payment Link" or "Mark Verified" buttons.
- **`renderPaymentDoneStage(isReadOnly)`** (For when lead reaches Policy Issued):
  - Will be prepared to accept `isReadOnly` to show the completed milestone trail without action buttons.

### CSS Additions
- Add new styles for the accordion container (`.accordion-section`, `.accordion-header`, `.accordion-content`, `.accordion-chevron`).
- Add utility classes for read-only forms (e.g., `.form-readonly` which applies `cursor: not-allowed`, muted backgrounds, and pointer-events nullification).

## Verification Plan

### Manual Verification
- Move a lead through all stages (New -> Contacted -> Quote -> Payment Done -> Policy Issued).
- Ensure that at each step, the previously completed stages appear as collapsed accordions above the active working area.
- Expand an accordion and verify all input fields, radio buttons, and quote actions are strictly read-only and no buttons appear.
- Verify multiple accordions can be opened simultaneously without exclusivity bugs.
args.Description: Create plan to refactor center panel to accordion history

<style>
/* Accordion styles */
.accordion-section {
    background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #E5E7EB; border-radius: 10px; overflow: hidden; margin-bottom: 12px;
}
.accordion-header {
    padding: 14px 18px; background: #F9FAFB; cursor: pointer; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between;
}
.accordion-header:hover {
    background: #F3F4F6;
}
.accordion-header-left {
    display: flex; align-items: center;
}
.accordion-stage-name {
    font-size: 13px; font-weight: 700; color: #111827;
}
.accordion-stage-summary {
    font-size: 11px; color: #6B7280; margin-left: 8px;
}
.accordion-chevron {
    color: #9CA3AF; transition: transform 0.2s; font-size: 14px;
}
.accordion-chevron.expanded {
    transform: rotate(180deg);
}
.accordion-content {
    padding: 18px; background: #ffffff; border-top: 1px solid #F3F4F6; display: none;
}
.accordion-content.open {
    display: block;
}
.form-readonly {
    background: #F9FAFB !important; border: 1px solid #F3F4F6 !important; cursor: not-allowed !important; pointer-events: none; opacity: 0.8;
}
